jobs.title: Banking Operations Officer
jobs.description:
• Main Responsibilities 

• The banking operations officer is responsible for the day-to-day activity, control and oversight of the BAU activity as directed by their reporting line. 

• The role requires the candidate to support the banking operations department across numerous activities as required by their manager, to deliver high quality standards of work. This presents the opportunity to demonstrate existing knowledge, in addition to being presented with the opportunity to upskill on other areas within the department. 

• It is critical to remain positive and proactive in this role, supporting colleagues and having a desire to increase their own knowledge and support others. 

• Support of constant improvement initiatives and projects will be required from time to time. 

• Role Duties 

• The local Banking Operation department is divided into two separate teams as detailed below : 
• Banking & Treasury 
• UK Domestic payments (Sterling) 

• Direct Debits & Standing orders 

• Cheque clearing 

• Treasury support activities 
 

• Client reporting, online banking & Debit cards 
• Client reporting, including valuations and mailings 

• Individual Savings Account controls - ISA 

• Client debit card support and helpline 

• Online banking support and helpline 

• Management of physical documents (mail / archives) 
 
 

• The successful candidate will initially join an area which makes best use of their skillset and experience, with a later consideration to crosstrain as required by the bank. 

• Important to support colleagues within banking operations and work with a one team approach. 

• Proactively manage Risks and Issues arising within the banking operations scope 

• Produce KPI’s for banking operations and provide periodic reporting against KPI’s for Senior Management as defined 

• Support the business with development of operational solutions for new products or services in conjunction with the Operational teams at Union Bancaire Privee SA 

• Ensure operational functions meet relevant regulatory requirements at all times 

• Perform all duties in accordance with the principles outlined in the UBP UK Code of Conduct, as well as the policies and procedures relevant to the role, adhering to a culture that focuses on minimising risks and delivering value 

• Ensure adherence to UBP Internal controls standards, systems and procedures that appropriate standards are maintained in all aspects of operational risk and compliance which are endorsed by subsequent audits. 
 

• The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. 

• Committee Membership 

• None 

• Conduct Rules 

• This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: 

• 

• To act with integrity; 

• To act with due skill, care and diligence; 

• To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; 

• To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; 

• To observe proper standards of market conduct; and 

• To act to deliver good outcomes for retain customers. 

• 

• Person Specification 

• Knowledge, Skills & Experience 

• At least 8 years working in a similar operational role, able to demonstrate technical knowledge across banking operations. 

• A min of 3 years working with critical outsource/service providers. 

• Able to engage at all levels and build strong relationships across jurisdictions. 

• Proactive, positive and solutions focused. 

• Competencies and Values Required Level 

• 

• Shows the drive to perform and be successful 5 
 Persistent in following up on issues 5 
 Upholds the legal, regulatory and ethical standard of the UBP Group 4 
 Good product knowledge 4 
 Knowledge of legal regulatory and industry requirements 4 
 Business Development 4 
 Communication - listening, written and verbal with clients 4 
 Influencing 4 
 Knowledge of Products and Services 4 
 Relationship Building - internal and external 4 
 Strategic & commercial awareness 4 
 Planning & organising 4 
 Ethics and values 4 
 Customer focus 4 
 

• Explanation: 1: low level of competence / skill required, 2-3: average level of competence / skill required, 4-5: high level of competence skill required 

•

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: IT Business Analyst - Risk Management
jobs.description:
• Mission 

• The mission consists in performing the role of IT business analyst for Risk applications, in particular for subjects related to Balance Sheet Management, Value At Risk (VaR), Reconciliation and IRRBB / CSRBB regulations. 

• The candidate needs to have an extensive IT risk management banking background as well as a strong BA experience. 
 

• Main responsibilities 

• Reporting to the Team Lead of IT Risk, Credit & ESG, the consultant will work on risk application projects. 
 
 
• IT BA Responsibilities 

• Analyse regulation, business scenarios/cases and contribute to business / regulatory requirements definition. 
• Eg : Impact of products evolution (Acumulator / Decumulator, Deposits, Credit and Mortgages) on the Balance Sheet Management (BSM) IT treatment 
 

• Define Functional Design Specification. 

• Manage vendor (FIS) application relationship (software package). 

• Execute database scripts and queries (SQL) to validate data integrity and ensure alignment with regulatory requirements. 

• Install and test new versions of BSM and VaR Hedge Funds Applications and exploit the applications in production (batch error management). 

• Liaise with the business to provide guidance from an IT point of view. 

• Provide guidance for business analysts, developers, and testers assigned to the projects and ensure good project management practices are employed throughout the project lifecycle. 

• Identify key risks and issues and define mitigation scenarios. 
 

 

• Your Profile 

• 5 to 10 years of experience 

• Education : Master Degree in IT 
 

• Experience required 

• IT Business Analyst with strong technical and functional skills on Risk Management – FIS Balance Sheet Manager – Var HF & Reconciliation (based on MATLAB) 

• Proficient in SQL and database scripting for risk data analytics. 

• Good understanding of the bank’s business processes 

• Good operational experience in the implementation of various IT Risk Management projects in banking or financial sectors related to risk management, VaR reconciliation, IRRBB (Interest Rate Risk in the Banking Book) / CSRBB (Credit Spread Risk in the Banking Book) regulations. 

• Good practrice of IT BA activities and knowledge of IT project management 
 

• Languages: 

• French mandatory and English (C1) 
 

• Others 

• Swiss residency: Optional

jobs.title: IT Business Analyst - Credit (IT)
jobs.description:
• Mission 

• The mission consists in performing the role of IT business analyst for key credit applications and projects 
 
 Main responsibilities: 

• Reporting to the Team Lead of IT Risk, Credit & ESG, the consultant will work on key credit applications projects. 
 

 BA Responsibilities: 
 
• Analyse business scenarios/cases and contribute to business requirements definition. 

• Define Functional Design Specification. 

• Manage vendor application (SpeciTec) relationship (software package Specicred ). 

• Install and test new version of Specicred. 

• Execute database scripts and queries (SQL) to validate data integrity and ensure alignment with regulatory requirements. 

• Liaise with the business to provide guidance from an IT point of view. 

• Provide guidance for business analysts, developers, and testers assigned to the projects and ensure good project management practices are employed throughout the project lifecycle. 

• Identify key risks and issues and define mitigation scenarios. 
 

• Your Profile 

• 5 to 10 years of experience 

• Education : Master Degree in IT 
 

• Experience required 

• Strong experience required: IT Business Analyst with strong technical and functional skills on Credit projects 

• Good understanding of the bank’s business processes 

• Good operational experience in the implementation of various IT Credit projects 
 

• Languages: 

• French mandatory and English (C1) 
 

• Others 

• Swiss residency: Mandatory

• Reporting to the Team Lead of IT Risk, Credit & ESG, the consultant will work on key credit applications projects. BA Responsibilities:
• Analyse business scenarios/cases and contribute to business requirements definition.
• Define Functional Design Specification.
• Manage vendor application (SpeciTec) relationship (software package Specicred ).
• Install and test new version of Specicred.
• Execute database scripts and queries (SQL) to validate data integrity and ensure alignment with regulatory requirements.
• Liaise with the business to provide guidance from an IT point of view.
• Provide guidance for business analysts, developers, and testers assigned to the projects and ensure good project management practices are employed throughout the project lifecycle.
• Identify key risks and issues and define mitigation scenarios.
• Your Profile
• 5 to 10 years of experience
• Education : Master Degree in IT
• Experience required
• Strong experience required: IT Business Analyst with strong technical and functional skills on Credit projects
• Good understanding of the bank’s business processes
• Good operational experience in the implementation of various IT Credit projects
• Languages:
• French mandatory and English (C1)
• Others
• Swiss residency: Mandatory

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Team leader - IT Compliance
jobs.description:
• Mission: 

• Team leader of the IT Compliance unit ensuring the delivery and operations of all the services used by the bank Compliance entities at Group and Branch levels 

• In charge of managing a team of 11 IT specialists composed of business analysts and project managers 
 

• Key responsibilities: 

• Manage the daily operations of the team and the related services to meet the expected level of quality 

• Manage the portfolio of projects and ensure the proper delivery on time by meeting the business requirements within the approved budget 

• Manage the team OPEX and CAPEX budgets within the approved envelopes 

• Manage the team resources by setting individual objectives and developing their career path 
 

• Specific Competencies and other Skills/Abilities: 

• Master Degree in IT Engineering or equivalent. 

• A minimum of 10 years’ experience as IT Team Lead in a banking organization 

• Functional knowledge of Compliance, KYC, AML and regulatory reporting topics in the context of Private Banking 

• Technical knowledge of the IMTF AML and risk scoring solutions 

• Capacity to deal with several matters simultaneously 

• Ability to work independently, take decisions and set priorities 

• Pragmatic and solution oriented 

• Strong leadership, analytical and communication skills, ability to interact at management level with key bank stakeholders 

• Fluent in French and English; additional languages are considered an asset 

• Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility 
 
 
• Others 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Middle Officer
jobs.description:
• Mission 

• The Middle Officer in Asset Management acts as the central point of contact for operational queries, ensuring timely client responses and smooth transaction processing. Key responsibilities include trade matching, settlement follow-up, daily reconciliations, collateral management, and issue resolution. The role also involves producing activity reports, collaborating with counterparties, and implementing new processes to enhance operational efficiency. 
 

• Main responsibilities 

• Act as the central point of contact for operational queries, and always available to respond to our client queries in a timely manner 

• Securities & FX trade matching, instruction to relevant third parties and settlement follow-up 

• OTC & ETD trade matching, and clearing monitoring 

• Produce the daily activity reports to third parties 

• Perform daily reconciliations of portfolio positions, cash balances and transactions. 

• Breaks analysis, investigation until resolution 

• Participation in the implementation of new processes and controls 

• Oversee the daily management of collateral for derivatives 

• Perform daily reconciliations of collateral balances with counterparties, resolving discrepancies in a timely manner. 

• Collaborate with counterparties, clearing brokers, and custodians to ensure smooth collateral movements and settlements. 
 

• Your Profile 

• +5 years of experience in Asset Management / Operations or FX activities 

• University degree(s) in accounting, finance and/or business management 

• Strong knowledge of fund structures, share class operations, and regulatory frameworks (e.g., EMIR, UCITS, AIFMD). 

• Deep understanding of currency markets, hedging instruments (e.g., forwards, swaps), and collateral management practices. 

• Specific competencies 
 
• Strong Analytical skills and problem-solving skills. 

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate across teams. 

• High attention to detail and ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment. 

• Awareness of risks and focus on risks mitigation techniques 

• Rigour and organization skills 

• Result oriented, driven to succeed 

• Ability to work in a dynamic, high demand environment 

• Fluent in French and English, other language(s) a plus 

• Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver results within tight deadlines. 

• Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and innovation. 

• Swiss residency 
 

 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Investment Advisor
jobs.description:
Mission 
 The Investment Advisor is responsible for delivering tailored investment advice and portfolio strategies to high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth individuals. Working closely with Relationship Managers (RMs), the IA ensures client receive comprehensive, risk-appropriate investment solutions aligned with their goals, profile, and the Bank’s values.
 
 Main responsibilities 
 
• Provide customized investment advice based on client objectives, risk appetite and market conditions

• Construct and optimize investment portfolios using a broad range of asset classes

• Present investment strategies clearly and transparently, including expected returns, risks and associated costs

• Conduct regular portfolio reviews and rebalancing recommendations

• Act as the trusted investment contact for existing clients, in partnership with RMs

• Support in deepening client relationships and enhancing retention through proactive investment insight and performance monitoring

• Assist in client meetings, prepare proposals and contribute to the acquisition and onboarding of new clients as well as for business retention

• Stay abreast of macroeconomic trends, financial markets and geopolitical developments

• Work closely with internal teams to align with the Bank’s investment strategy and core values

• Promote and explain investment products to RMs and relevant target advisory client groups

• Ensure full compliance with Swiss regulatory frameworks
 

 Your Profile 
 
• University degree in Finance, economics or related field, or a qualification considered equivalent 

• CFA, CIIA, CWMA or similar certification is a strong asset 

• Strong knowledge of investment products and ability to execute trades for equities, derivatives, fixed income, bonds, warrants and mutual funds 

• Min 5 years of experience in a similar position in Private Banking or a Financial Institution 

• Strong communication and interpersonal skills, client focus, reliability, flexibility and stress resistant 

• Good command of MS Office suite, portfolio analysis tools and banking software 

• Perfectly fluent in French and English. Very good knowledge of Russian and any additional language skill would be an added value. 

• Adaptability to work in an agile organization 
 
 Others 
 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Corporate Actions Specialist (6 months)
jobs.description:
TEMPORARY MISSION - 6 MONTHS
 
• Mission 
 Ensure the accurate and timely processing of income payments for all bank accounts while maintaining compliance with regulatory requirements and internal controls. Provide exceptional service to clients and stakeholders, ensuring smooth operations within the income processing function.
 
• Main responsibilities 
 Income Processing 
 
• Process income-related transactions, including dividends, interest payments, and other distributions.

• Ensure accuracy and timeliness in accordance with established procedures and regulations.
 Reconciliation and Verification 
 
• Reconcile and verify income transactions, ensuring correct allocation to client accounts.

• Investigate and resolve discrepancies or exceptions.

• Manage market claims and liaise with external brokers and counterparties.
 Client Communication 
 
• Address inquiries from Relationship Managers, internal stakeholders, and external parties (e.g., custodians, issuers, agents).

• Provide updates and maintain a high level of professionalism and customer service.
 Documentation and Recordkeeping 
 
• Maintain accurate records of income transactions and related documentation.

• Ensure proper documentation for audit and regulatory purposes.
 Regulatory Compliance 
 
• Stay informed on relevant regulations, industry practices, and internal policies
 

• Your Profile 

• Languages: French, English 
 
 Personal skills: 
 
• Think in "Problem-Action-Results" terms 

• Be proactive and driven 

• Show strong client service ethic 

• Maintain quality standards despite schedule pressures 

• Highly organized, manage tight deadlines within a high-risk environment, ability to work under pressure 

• Strong analytical skills with an ability to identify problems and propose solutions. 

• Express opinions and criticism in a constructive way 

• Be a continuous learner 

• Adaptability, self-motivation and teamwork 

• Good understanding of the risks inherent to the corporate actions environment and demonstrate good discipline around control and escalation. 

• Build lasting client relationships 

• Consistently comply with internal & external regulations 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

descriptionByLocale.it:
TEMPORARY MISSION - 6 MONTHS
• Mission Ensure the accurate and timely processing of income payments for all bank accounts while maintaining compliance with regulatory requirements and internal controls. Provide exceptional service to clients and stakeholders, ensuring smooth operations within the income processing function.
• Main responsibilities Income Processing
• Process income-related transactions, including dividends, interest payments, and other distributions.
• Ensure accuracy and timeliness in accordance with established procedures and regulations. Reconciliation and Verification
• Reconcile and verify income transactions, ensuring correct allocation to client accounts.
• Investigate and resolve discrepancies or exceptions.
• Manage market claims and liaise with external brokers and counterparties. Client Communication
• Address inquiries from Relationship Managers, internal stakeholders, and external parties (e.g., custodians, issuers, agents).
• Provide updates and maintain a high level of professionalism and customer service. Documentation and Recordkeeping
• Maintain accurate records of income transactions and related documentation.
• Ensure proper documentation for audit and regulatory purposes. Regulatory Compliance
• Stay informed on relevant regulations, industry practices, and internal policies
• Your Profile
• Languages: French, English Personal skills:
• Think in "Problem-Action-Results" terms
• Be proactive and driven
• Show strong client service ethic
• Maintain quality standards despite schedule pressures
• Highly organized, manage tight deadlines within a high-risk environment, ability to work under pressure
• Strong analytical skills with an ability to identify problems and propose solutions.

• Build lasting client relationships

jobs.title: Assistant Relationship Manager Emerging Markets
jobs.description:
Mission 
 Assist the relationship manager(s) in opening new relationships and serving clients, in compliance with 
 the Bank’s internal regulations and guidelines within the Eastern Europe region. 
 
 Main responsibilities 
 
• Prepare the necessary documentation for client meetings and business trips for relationship managers 

• Execute and monitor stock exchange orders, forex transactions, fiduciary relationships, loans, payment orders and credit card applications 

• Provide administrative support to the Team Head 

• Cash Management 

• Assist in AML clarifications and prepare clients KYC and related updates with direct line contact 

• Direct interaction with clients by phone, e-mail and, occasionally in meetings 

• Prepare and monitor account openings, closings and mutations 

• Maintain records in compliance with internal and external regulation (for example: portfolio reviews, AML, KYC, etc.) 

• Perform other administrative tasks as needed (for example: preparing presentations, travel arrangements, helping clients with any requests, banking and non-banking, etc.) 

• Deputy of other assistants if needed 
 

 Your Profile 
 
• Degree in a related field (banking apprenticeship, CYP, banking internship or similar) 

• Private Banking certificate, CWMA or other equivalent certification a plus 

• Minimum 5 years of experience in a similar role in a Private Bank 

• Strong banking and KYC knowledge 

• Strong team spirit, inter-personal skills, open-minded, highly service and client oriented as well as excellent communication skills 

• Fluent in French and English. Russian would be a strong added value 
 
 Others 
 
• Swiss Residence mandatory 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Structured Product Engineer
jobs.description:
Mission & Main responsibilities 
 
• Product Management: 
• Oversee the booking process for new structured products and ensure effective life cycle monitoring of existing products.

• Manage post-trade services for the EMEA team, including the creation of products in internal systems and mapping products into team tools.

• Act as a liaison between Bankers, Advisors, Middle Office, and Back Office teams to ensure seamless communication and coordination.
 
 
 
• Digital Development and Process Optimization: 
• Identify opportunities for improvement in both automated and manual tasks, providing actionable feedback and suggestions.

• Design, develop, and maintain Excel-based tools to support the Structured Products team in their daily operations.

• Contribute to the enhancement of digital processes to improve efficiency and accuracy in team workflows.

• Contribute to automation of the booking process.
 
 
 
• Ensure the best execution in a secure framework in open architecture 
• Pricing of structured products in a secure operational framework

• Focus on flow products (in comparison to solution products)

• Ensure best execution in accordance with regulatory requirements

• Management of the Issuer relationship, with a focus on the search for internal group synergies

• Back up of the Solutions activity in case of needs
 
 
 
• Development of tactical trade ideas 
• Design, analysis, and selection of SP tactical ideas (trading axis, market opportunities etc.)

• Promotion of new payoffs within subscription products
 
 
 
• Client focus: 
• Provide expertise and support to internal partners and clients

• Take initiatives to achieve quantitative objectives for the structured products business

• Build relationship with private bankers

• Favour internal synergies
 
 
 Your Profile 
 
• Education: Master degree in Financial Markets

• Experience Technical skills - 5y of experience on Structured Products field

• Languages - English, French, German, others

• Personal skills - Team player, agile, Pro active, ability to take initiatives, business developpement & service oriented

• IT skills: Programming proficiency with at least one major language, such as Python. Excellent knowledge of the Pandas and NumPy packages. Proven experience in project development or automation using this expertise / data management and data analysis.

• Others: Swiss Residence
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Securities Master File Specialist
jobs.description:
• Job Summary 

• The Securities Master File Specialist is a critical role within the bank, responsible for managing and maintaining the integrity of financial instrument data across Wealth Management and Asset Management businesses. This role ensures the accurate and timely setup, maintenance, and monitoring of securities and benchmarks, supporting front office teams, risk management, operations, and client reporting. 

• In addition to the core responsibilities, the Securities Master File Specialist will play a pivotal role in the project to replace the current tool managing the securities master file . This includes contributing significantly to the success of the project by actively participating in testing, data migration validation, and supporting the adoption of the new platform. 

• The ideal candidate will have a strong attention to detail, excellent analytical skills, and the ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders. The role also involves active participation in project management initiatives to enhance processes, systems, and data quality. 
 

• Key Responsibilities 

• Securities and Prices Management 

• Open and update financial instruments in the database, ensuring accuracy and completeness. 

• Process requests for new instrument setups from various business units, including Private Banking, Asset Management, Treasury, and Trading. 

• Conduct thorough analysis and research of additional information (e.g., prospectuses, offering documents, term sheets, and data providers). 

• Enter and maintain additional information in the Instruments Database. 

• Manage and monitor prices and Net Asset Values (NAV) per share. 

• Perform regular controls to ensure data quality and integrity. 

• Data Quality Assurance 

• Execute daily, weekly, and monthly processes and controls to maintain a high level of data quality service. 

• Ensure data accuracy to support front office teams, risk management, and operations. 

• Participate in the implementation of new procedures, projects, and controls. 

• Document all processes with operational procedures (including process flows, risks, controls, and key performance indicators). 
 

• Role in the Securities Master File tool replacement project 

• Testing and Validation: 
• Actively participate in defining and executing test cases for the new platform. 

• Analyze test results, identify anomalies, and collaborate with project teams to resolve issues. 

• Ensure the new tool meets functional and operational requirements. 
 

• Data Migration and Quality Control: 
• Validate the accuracy and completeness of data migrated to the new platform. 

• Perform detailed checks to identify and address discrepancies in the migrated data. 
 

• Change Management and User Support: 
 
• Contribute to the development of user guides, training materials, and process documentation for the new tool. 

• Provide training and support to end-users to ensure a smooth transition to the new platform. 

• Act as a key point of contact for feedback and troubleshooting during the implementation phase. 
 

• Core competencies 

• Behave Ethically 

• Communicate Effectively 

• Foster Teamwork 

• Creativity/Innovation 

• Organize 

• Plan 

• Lead / Manage 

• Make Decisions 

• Solve Problems 

 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

• In addition to the core responsibilities, the Securities Master File Specialist will play a pivotal role in the project to replace the current tool managing the securities master file . This includes contributing significantly to the success of the project by actively participating in testing, data migration validation, and supporting the adoption of the new platform.
• The ideal candidate will have a strong attention to detail, excellent analytical skills, and the ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders. The role also involves active participation in project management initiatives to enhance processes, systems, and data quality.
• Key Responsibilities
• Securities and Prices Management
• Open and update financial instruments in the database, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
• Process requests for new instrument setups from various business units, including Private Banking, Asset Management, Treasury, and Trading.
• Conduct thorough analysis and research of additional information (e.g., prospectuses, offering documents, term sheets, and data providers).
• Enter and maintain additional information in the Instruments Database.
• Manage and monitor prices and Net Asset Values (NAV) per share.
• Perform regular controls to ensure data quality and integrity.
• Data Quality Assurance
• Execute daily, weekly, and monthly processes and controls to maintain a high level of data quality service.
• Ensure data accuracy to support front office teams, risk management, and operations.
• Participate in the implementation of new procedures, projects, and controls.
• Document all processes with operational procedures (including process flows, risks, controls, and key performance indicators).
• Role in the Securities Master File tool replacement project
• Testing and Validation:
• Actively participate in defining and executing test cases for the new platform.
• Analyze test results, identify anomalies, and collaborate with project teams to resolve issues.
• Ensure the new tool meets functional and operational requirements.
• Data Migration and Quality Control:
• Validate the accuracy and completeness of data migrated to the new platform.
• Perform detailed checks to identify and address discrepancies in the migrated data.
• Change Management and User Support:
• Contribute to the development of user guides, training materials, and process documentation for the new tool.
• Provide training and support to end-users to ensure a smooth transition to the new platform.
• Act as a key point of contact for feedback and troubleshooting during the implementation phase.
• Core competencies
• Behave Ethically
• Communicate Effectively
• Foster Teamwork
• Creativity/Innovation
• Organize
• Plan
• Lead / Manage
• Make Decisions
• Solve Problems Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Team Head Cash Services

titleByLocale.it: Servizi di cassa a capo squadra

jobs.description:
• Mission 
 Within the COO division, part of the Group Operations, the Cash Services Department covers the following areas:
- Transfers & payments; 
- Cash counter, Clients’ safes and Client care center (CCC); 
- The Money Market & FX transactions; 
- The Cards activity. 
The mission of the department is to make sure that the bank’s, contractual or regulatory, obligations are fulfilled, in line with its policies and procedures, especially in a more and more strict and complex environment but also bearing in mind the excellence in the service to clients.
The mission of the Head of Cash Services is to provide proper guidance and support to the teams to achieve this goal.

 
 
• Main responsibilities 
• Coordinate and organise the work of the department 
• Delegate appropriately the tasks to each team leader.

• Provide guidance based on the top management direction.

• Make sure, with a clear communication of the bank’s policies and procedures, that a correct risk assessment is made and, if need be, escalation is timely done according to the level of risk.
 

• Implement relevant monitoring and controls framework 
• Identify any risk (operational and regulatory)

• Identify, implement and report quantitative and qualitative reporting

• Challenge procedures and routines. 
 

• Coordinate with internal or external counterparties 
• Act as an advisor and coordinator vis-à-vis branches and subsidiaries across the Group, sharing best practices, standardising processes and controls when possible. 

• Maintain good relationship with all stakeholders of the bank to provide support and advise. 

• Develop a network of professionals with other institutions to keep abreast of systems and regulations evolutions. 
 

• Continuously improve the set up 
• Be actively involved in all projects impacting the department from TOM definition to tests and implementation 

• Propose measures to increase efficiency, quality and productivity.
 

• Provide team leadership and coaching 
• Lead by example: behaviour consistent with words

• Adopt change with a positive attitude. Express opinions and criticism in a constructive way

• Communicate effectively and be able to take decisions including in difficult situations 

• Coach and help develop team members: identify good and low performers and take appropriate actions to develop them or solve dysfunctional behaviour
 
 
 
 
 
• Your Profile 
• Strong knowledge in Cash Services activities and processes 

• MS Office and major banking software

• Experience and proven success in running similar position 

• English is a requirement, other languages a plus
 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Tax Specialist
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Tax Specialist to join our Client Documentation and Tax Services Department.
As part of the Fiscal Reporting team, you will play a key role in ensuring the accuracy and quality of our clients’ tax statements, while collaborating with various departments and external providers.
This is a unique opportunity to grow in a fast-paced, stimulating environment and contribute to impactful projects across the bank.
 
 
• Main responsibilities 
• Tax Statement Review & Validation 
• Review and validate tax statements for over 20 international jurisdictions, including Switzerland, France, Portugal, Mexico, and others.

• Ensure that tax documents comply with local and international regulations before being sent to clients.
 

• Data Quality Assurance 
• Collaborate with our external provider to ensure the quality and accuracy of tax data before statement production.

• Identify and correct anomalies or inconsistencies in the data to ensure reliable reporting.
 

• Client Support & Front Office Interaction 
• Address inquiries and requests from the Front Office regarding tax statements, providing clear and precise explanations.

• Manage tax document orders in coordination with internal teams and external providers.
 

• Cross-Functional Collaboration 
• Work closely with Compliance, Operations, Front Office, and IT teams to ensure smooth management of tax processes.

• Actively participate in cross-functional projects involving process improvements, regulatory updates, or strategic initiatives.
 

• Project Management 
• Contribute to side projects related to tax data, information systems, and operational processes.

• Participate in the continuous improvement of tools and processes in collaboration with IT and other departments.
 

• Regulatory Compliance & Reporting 
• Stay updated on tax regulatory changes across the covered jurisdictions.

• Prepare detailed reports and maintain comprehensive documentation to meet audit and regulatory requirements.
 
 
 

• Your Profile 
• Experience : Minimum 2 years in banking, with a focus on tax services.

• Skills : Strong knowledge of financial instruments (Equities, Bonds, Structured Products, Derivatives – Listed or OTC) and their specific characteristics. Familiarity with European income and gains taxation is a plus. Proficiency in Microsoft Office is essential.

• Languages : Fluency in French and English is required; German, Italian, Spanish, or Portuguese is a plus.

• Mindset : Proactive, solution-oriented, and eager to learn. Strong problem-solving skills, ability to work quickly and efficiently, and a team player who thrives in a fast-paced environment.
 
 

• Why Join Us 
• Be part of a collaborative and innovative team.

• Work in a challenging yet rewarding environment.

• Develop your expertise and grow your career in a leading financial institution.

 
 

• 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Help Desk Officer - 1st level of support

titleByLocale.it: Help Desk Officer - 1° livello di supporto
titleByLocale.en: Help Desk Officer - 1st level of support
titleByLocale.de: Help Desk Officer - 1st level of support
titleByLocale.fr: Help Desk Officer - 1st level of support

jobs.description:
Mission 
Based in our main office in Geneva, you will join the Global Service Desk team, providing first-line support to approximately 3,000 Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) colleagues worldwide.
 
 Main responsibilities 
 
• Answer to the Global Service Desk phone line for all UBP users worldwide.

• Manage emails sent to Global Service Desk.

• Manage workload from our self-ticketing tool.

• Provide remote assistance to users and try to resolve the problem on first call.

• Create incidents with clear and explicit information and assign to level 2 teams if necessary.

• Knowledge documentation and procedures updates.

• Working hours: Weekdays, shift coverage from 07:30 to 19:00.
 
 Your Profile 
 
• Help Desk Institute Certification (CFC) or equivalent.

• A first successful experience in a Help Desk service is mandatory.

• Microsoft Operating System Certification(s) will be an asset.

• ITIL Foundation Certification will be an asset.

• Expertise in Microsoft Windows 11 and Microsoft 365 (Office 365)

• Experience with Citrix and VDI environments and with Remote Access connection (RSA Secure ID, Citrix Workspace).

• Knowledge of, Active Directory, GPO, SCCM, Networking.

• Knowledge on Mobility: iOS and iPhone, iPad.

• JIRA service management tool knowledge.

• Fluent in French and English; additional languages considered an asset.
 
 Competencies 
 
• Excellent analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.

• Excellent oral and written communication skills.

• Highly customer service oriented.

• Priority setting, multitask ability.

• Team player willing to take ownership.

• Ability to work in a fast-changing environment.
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Head of Internal Audit
jobs.description:
Job description 
 Head of Internal Audit, UBP UK 
 
 

 NAME: 
 
 
 CORPORATE JOB TITLE: 
 Head of Internal Audit, London 
 
 CORPORATE TITLE: 
 TBC 
 
 REPORTS TO: 
 CEO, UBP UK & 
 Group Head of Internal Audit & 
 Head of the Audit Committee (Member of the Board of Directors) 
 
 SENIOR MANAGEMENT FUNCTION (YES/NO): 
 Yes 
 
 PRESCRIBED RESPONSBILITY: 
 SMF 5 
 
 MATERIAL RISK TAKER (YES/NO): 
 Yes 
 
 CERTIFIED (YES/NO): 
 No 
 
 CERTIFIED FUNCTIONS: 
 
 
 
 LAST UPDATED: 
 March 2026 
 
 Main Responsibilities 
 This role will hold overall responsibility for Internal Audit across the UBP UK, providing risk-based objective assurance, advice and insight. UBP Internal Audit follows the standards of the profession, as well as the regulatory requirements and UBP Group policies and procedures. 
 The Head of Internal Audit is responsible for independent and objective audits reviews of all UBP UK activities, aiming at providing the Group with assurance as to how effectively it is controlling its operations, advises on improvements. The role also assesses the quality of risk management within an audited scope, the appropriateness and effectiveness of the permanent control framework, the risk sensitivity of management and compliance with codes of conduct and expected professional practices. 
 Role Duties 
 
• The Head of Audit is responsible for managing, planning, and administering the internal audit function in the UK, Channel Islands and Gibraltar regions for all activities of UBP UK. 

• Plan, supervise and take part in audits across all activities of UBP UK including financial, regulatory, compliance, operational audits as needed. 

• Develop and maintain good relationships with the head and key management personnel of all activity pillars across UBP UK. 

• Make recommendations for organisational and internal controls improvements of UBP UK. 

• Prepare Audit reports, and summaries of findings and audit reports and recommendations to the Group Audit department, as well as key management stakeholders of UBP UK. 

• Follow-up of Audit Recommendations until their implementation. 

• Make recommendations for improving the efficiency and effectiveness of the audit process. 

• Managing and engaging with various stakeholders across all departments of UBP UK at a senior level. 

• Liaise with external auditors and regulators. 
 
 
 The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. 
 
 Management and Supervision 
 
• Current line management responsibility for one Internal Audit Manager. 

• Set goals and objectives for all staff under direct supervision, complete performance appraisals and arrange appraisal interviews and ensure that the UBP UK guidelines are appropriately updated and communicated to staff. 
 
 Statements of Responsibility 
 Prescribed Responsibilities and Local Responsibilities for the Head of Internal Audit are set out in the Statements of Responsibility which is maintained as a separate document. 
 
 Conduct Rules 
 This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 
• To act with integrity; 

• To act with due skill, care and diligence; 

• To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; 

• To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; 

• To observe proper standards of market conduct; and 

• To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. 
 
 In addition, this role must also comply with the Senior Manager Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 
• To take reasonable steps to ensure that the business of the firm for which you are responsible is controlled effectively. 

• To take reasonable steps to ensure that the business of the firm for which you are responsible complies with the relevant requirements and standards of the regulatory system. 

• To take reasonable steps to ensure that any delegation of your responsibilities is to an appropriate person and that you oversee the discharge of the delegated responsibility effectively. 

• To disclose appropriately any information of which the FCA or PRA would reasonably expect notice. 
 
 Person Specification 
 Knowledge, Skills & Experience 
 
 Qualifications 
 
• Qualifications: Professional Audit/Accounting qualification or Degree is preferred (ACA, ACCA, CIA, etc.) 

• Good knowledge of accounting principles and professional audit standards. 

• Solid experience at a Head of Internal Audit or Senior Audit Manager level with an audit firm or a financial institution. 

• Working experience in both wealth management (essential) and asset management industries (preferred). 

• Experience managing audits across a broad range of activities, including financial, compliance, regulatory, operational, systems, etc. 

• Able to influence senior stakeholders whilst maintaining objectivity and independence 

• Written skills for preparing and delivering quality audit reports, as well as strong relationship building and communication skills to ensure the best outcome. 

• Knowledge of UK banking legislation, regulations and rules (PRA/FCA) 

• Excellent communicator and able to build relationships with internal and external stakeholders including regulators 

• Ability to influence senior stakeholders 

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to assess complex situations and make sound decisions. 

• Demonstrated experience in developing and implementing compliance policies, and procedures. 

• On-going CPD (Continuous Professional Development) to ensure that adequate knowledge of the UK regulatory environment is maintained. 

• Suitably qualified to hold SMF 5. 
 
 Competencies and Values Required Level 
 
 Shows the drive to perform and be successful 5 
 Persistent in following-up and through on issues 5 
 Upholds the legal, regulatory and ethical standard of the UBP Group 5 
 Good product knowledge 4 
 Knowledge of legal regulatory and industry requirements 5 
 Business Development 3 
 Communication - listening, written and verbal with clients 5 
 Influencing 5 
 Relationship Building - internal and external 5 
 Strategic & commercial awareness 4 
 Planning & organising 4 
 Ethics and values 5 
 Customer focus 5 
 Explanation: 1: low level of competence / skill required, 2-3: average level of competence / skill required, 4-5: high level of competence skill required

• Prepare Audit reports, and summaries of findings and audit reports and recommendations to the Group Audit department, as well as key management stakeholders of UBP UK.
• Follow-up of Audit Recommendations until their implementation.
• Make recommendations for improving the efficiency and effectiveness of the audit process.
• Managing and engaging with various stakeholders across all departments of UBP UK at a senior level.
• Liaise with external auditors and regulators. The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. Management and Supervision
• Current line management responsibility for one Internal Audit Manager.
• Set goals and objectives for all staff under direct supervision, complete performance appraisals and arrange appraisal interviews and ensure that the UBP UK guidelines are appropriately updated and communicated to staff. Statements of Responsibility Prescribed Responsibilities and Local Responsibilities for the Head of Internal Audit are set out in the Statements of Responsibility which is maintained as a separate document. Conduct Rules This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are:
• To act with integrity;
• To act with due skill, care and diligence;
• To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators;
• To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly;
• To observe proper standards of market conduct; and
• To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. In addition, this role must also comply with the Senior Manager Conduct Rules, which are:
• To take reasonable steps to ensure that the business of the firm for which you are responsible is controlled effectively.
• To take reasonable steps to ensure that the business of the firm for which you are responsible complies with the relevant requirements and standards of the regulatory system.
• To take reasonable steps to ensure that any delegation of your responsibilities is to an appropriate person and that you oversee the discharge of the delegated responsibility effectively.
• To disclose appropriately any information of which the FCA or PRA would reasonably expect notice. Person Specification Knowledge, Skills & Experience Qualifications
• Qualifications: Professional Audit/Accounting qualification or Degree is preferred (ACA, ACCA, CIA, etc.)
• Good knowledge of accounting principles and professional audit standards.
• Solid experience at a Head of Internal Audit or Senior Audit Manager level with an audit firm or a financial institution.
• Working experience in both wealth management (essential) and asset management industries (preferred).
• Experience managing audits across a broad range of activities, including financial, compliance, regulatory, operational, systems, etc.
• Able to influence senior stakeholders whilst maintaining objectivity and independence
• Written skills for preparing and delivering quality audit reports, as well as strong relationship building and communication skills to ensure the best outcome.
• Knowledge of UK banking legislation, regulations and rules (PRA/FCA)
• Excellent communicator and able to build relationships with internal and external stakeholders including regulators
• Ability to influence senior stakeholders
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to assess complex situations and make sound decisions.
• Demonstrated experience in developing and implementing compliance policies, and procedures.
• On-going CPD (Continuous Professional Development) to ensure that adequate knowledge of the UK regulatory environment is maintained.
• Suitably qualified to hold SMF 5. Competencies and Values Required Level Shows the drive to perform and be successful 5 Persistent in following-up and through on issues 5 Upholds the legal, regulatory and ethical standard of the UBP Group 5 Good product knowledge 4 Knowledge of legal regulatory and industry requirements 5 Business Development 3 Communication - listening, written and verbal with clients 5 Influencing 5 Relationship Building - internal and external 5 Strategic & commercial awareness 4 Planning & organising 4 Ethics and values 5 Customer focus 5 Explanation: 1: low level of competence / skill required, 2-3: average level of competence / skill required, 4-5: high level of competence skill required

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Facilities Officer (Temporary)
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 Veiller à la maintenance et à la sécurité des locaux tout en assurant le bien-être de leurs occupants.
Capacité à trouver des solutions efficaces et pragmatiques répondant aux besoins techniques des utilisateurs
 
 
• Main responsibilities 
• Répondre aux demandes des collaborateurs sur les problématiques liées aux locaux et équipements.

• Effectuer les petites réparations et interventions de maintenance de premier niveau.

• Contrôler l'état général des bâtiments et signaler les anomalies.

• Coordonner et accompagner les prestataires lors des interventions en s'assurant du respect des règles de sécurité de la banque.

• Vérifier visuellement les installations techniques et remonter les différents problèmes.

• Participer à l'organisation des espaces de travail.

• Effectuer des déménagements internes et des mises en place de mobiliers.

• Participer à la gestion des stocks de fournitures et passer les commandes validées par le responsable.

• Vérifier le bon fonctionnement des équipements de sécurité.

• Appliquer les consignes de sécurité de la banque.

• Participer au piquet technique.
 
 

• Your Profile 
• Expérience attendue de Facilities officer en interne ou dans une société de service. 

• Connaissances en bâtiment. 
o Electricité : vérification des installations et relamping. 
o Plomberie : Débouchage de canalisations simples et entretien des sanitaires. 
o Serrurerie/Menuiserie : Ajustement portes des fenêtres et du mobilier, changement des serrures. 
o Revêtement : remplacement de revêtement sols.

• Polyvalence et autonomie dans l'exécution des tâches. 

• Réactivité et capacité à gérer les urgences. 

• Sens du service et excellent relationnel. 

• Rigueur et respect des consignes. 
 
 

• Background 
• High School/ Maturité / Baccalauréat 

• Connaissances en sécurité incendie et accessibilité des bâtiments. 

• Excel, GMAO, Ticketing. 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Graduate Programme - Operations office team
jobs.description:
The Bank’s entire operations are managed by COO Division and are headed up by the Chief Operating Officer. It strives to provide both in-house and external clients with a first-rate service to ensure our business operates efficiently and at the best possible cost. The COO division comprises Operations, Finance, Information Management, Human Resources and Logistics. COO employees are committed to providing, with professionalism and enthusiasm, an efficient, proactive, high-quality service that meets the needs of clients within the context of the Bank’s overall strategy. 
 
• Mission : 

• Permanent contract and training programme of 12 months, based on a rotation through various departments of the COO team 

• The purpose is to acquire targeted professional skills as well as practical experience 

• You will learn to manage projects, build stakeholder relationships, and conduct change management 

• You will be supported by a mentor, and a “buddy” during the entire programme 

• This programme is dedicated to young graduates, with a Master’s degree and a maximum of 12 months’ global professional experience, seeking to discover the Banking industry 

• It includes the prospect of obtaining a permanent role within UBP Operations Team 
 

• Main responsibilities : 
 1. Operations – Monitoring and Reconciliations 
 
• Understand the role of the Operations department, and the roles of the different teams within Operations 

• Understand Operations processes and propose process improvements where applicable 

• Participate in coordination tasks of transversal topics / projects within Operations department 

• Help to realise reportings and collect inputs from other teams on regular reportings 

• Understand and support Reconciliation’s activity and investigations 
 

 2. Banking Operations 
 
• Join an Operations team 

• Understand the role of the Operations department, and the roles of the different teams within Operations 

• Understand Operations processes and propose process improvements where applicable 

• Support the processes of the teams in line with internal procedures and policies 

• Support the cash services and securities services by discovering transfers, treasury operations, stock exchange and settlement activities. 

 
 3. Risk management department 
 
• Join the risk management team 

• Understand the different roles within the risk team 

• Understand Operational risk going through incident management, RCSA… 

• Support risk processes 

 
 
• Your Profile : 

• Master’s degree in finance or Information Technology obtained recently at a highly recognised university 

• Maximum 12 months’ professional experience 

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, ability to partner with other teams 

• Fluent in French and English; any other language would be a plus 

• Excellent knowledge of the Microsoft Office suite especially Excel, programming skills is a plus 

• Strong critical thinking, analytical and problem-solving skills 

• Team player 

 
 
• Others : 
• Swiss Residence
 
 
 Applications : 
 Applications must be made through our careers website and must include a CV and a cover letter. 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team

titleByLocale.it: Programma di laurea – Gruppo Risk Management team
titleByLocale.en: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team
titleByLocale.de: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team
titleByLocale.fr: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team

jobs.description:
The Group’s risk management activities are overseen by Group Risk Management, led by the Group Head of Risk Management (Chief Risk Officer / CRO). In Switzerland, Group Risk Management teams are responsible for overseeing the Bank’s activities, while ensuring local alignment with Group standards at an international level. They coordinate methodologies, implement transversal risk management initiatives and lead key local projects in close collaboration with Group-level risk representatives. The Swiss Risk Management function is structured into specialized teams covering Treasury & Trading, Asset Management, Wealth Management and Operational Risk. In addition a dedicated Project team provides cross-functional support for key initiatives led by the CRO or team heads. Acting as a second line of defense, the Risk teams serve as trusted advisors to the Business, participate to local committees, perform independent controls and support the definition and implementation of mitigation plans in coordination with risk owners (1st line of defense).
 
 Mission 
As of 1 September 2026, Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is offering a permanent position within its Group Risk Management teams (2nd line of defense) in Geneva, starting with a 12‑month training program that includes regular interactions with other Bank functions and Legal Entity representatives across the Group.
The purpose is to acquire targeted professional skills as well as practical experience with a strong orientation on risk management and secondly on project management.
 
• Rotations: The program includes four, three-month rotations across different teams.
 

• Main responsibilities 
 
 
 During the program, under the supervision of the team managers in their role of mentor, the Graduate is expected to provide support on all teams’ activities as well as assist them in their daily tasks, with in particular:

 
 
• Gain hands-on experience in the oversight of control framework and risk assessments across Wealth Management activities

• Build a strong foundation in risk management framework and contribute to the continuous enhancement of risk and control methodologies

• Perform control activities and deliver clear and insightful analysis to internal stakeholders

• Get involved in projects and support their implementation

• Contribute to thematic reviews and identify value-added improvement opportunities

• Contribute to the improvement of risk management reporting to Senior Management and communication methods with the 1 LoD (the Business) through innovative, technology-driven enhancements.
 
 During the program, you will also have the possibility to start a certification related to Banking sector and/or your function, for examples the CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) or CIA (Certified Internal Auditor), further enhancing your professional development.
 Your Profile 
 Background 
 
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in finance, business management recently obtained from a highly recognised university or equivalent.

• Maximum 12-months’ professional experience.
 Knowledges 

• Good understanding of banking / financial sector, interest for Private Banking model and challenge / regulations.

• Knowledge of risk management and/or control activities an expectation. 

• Experience in project management a plus.

• Strong analytical skills, strong presentation skills.
 Technical Skills 

• Excellent knowledge of the Microsoft Office tools especially Excel, PowerPoint.

• Knowledge in Power BI a plus.

• Fluent in English and French (minimum C1 in both).
 Specific Competencies 

• High motivation, attention to details, excellent organization skills and strong attention to deadline 

• Discretion as a need-to-have

descriptionByLocale.it:
The Group’s risk management activities are overseen by Group Risk Management, led by the Group Head of Risk Management (Chief Risk Officer / CRO). In Switzerland, Group Risk Management teams are responsible for overseeing the Bank’s activities, while ensuring local alignment with Group standards at an international level. They coordinate methodologies, implement transversal risk management initiatives and lead key local projects in close collaboration with Group-level risk representatives. The Swiss Risk Management function is structured into specialized teams covering Treasury & Trading, Asset Management, Wealth Management and Operational Risk. In addition a dedicated Project team provides cross-functional support for key initiatives led by the CRO or team heads. Acting as a second line of defense, the Risk teams serve as trusted advisors to the Business, participate to local committees, perform independent controls and support the definition and implementation of mitigation plans in coordination with risk owners (1st line of defense). Mission As of 1 September 2026, Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is offering a permanent position within its Group Risk Management teams (2nd line of defense) in Geneva, starting with a 12‑month training program that includes regular interactions with other Bank functions and Legal Entity representatives across the Group. The purpose is to acquire targeted professional skills as well as practical experience with a strong orientation on risk management and secondly on project management.
• Rotations: The program includes four, three-month rotations across different teams.
• Main responsibilities During the program, under the supervision of the team managers in their role of mentor, the Graduate is expected to provide support on all teams’ activities as well as assist them in their daily tasks, with in particular:
• Gain hands-on experience in the oversight of control framework and risk assessments across Wealth Management activities
• Build a strong foundation in risk management framework and contribute to the continuous enhancement of risk and control methodologies
• Perform control activities and deliver clear and insightful analysis to internal stakeholders
• Get involved in projects and support their implementation
• Contribute to thematic reviews and identify value-added improvement opportunities
• Contribute to the improvement of risk management reporting to Senior Management and communication methods with the 1 LoD (the Business) through innovative, technology-driven enhancements. During the program, you will also have the possibility to start a certification related to Banking sector and/or your function, for examples the CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) or CIA (Certified Internal Auditor), further enhancing your professional development. Your Profile Background
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in finance, business management recently obtained from a highly recognised university or equivalent.
• Maximum 12-months’ professional experience. Knowledges
• Good understanding of banking / financial sector, interest for Private Banking model and challenge / regulations.
• Knowledge of risk management and/or control activities an expectation.
• Experience in project management a plus.
• Strong analytical skills, strong presentation skills. Technical Skills
• Excellent knowledge of the Microsoft Office tools especially Excel, PowerPoint.
• Knowledge in Power BI a plus.
• Fluent in English and French (minimum C1 in both). Specific Competencies
• High motivation, attention to details, excellent organization skills and strong attention to deadline
• Discretion as a need-to-have
• Multi-tasking and solutions oriented
• Very strong team spirit and capable to work independently
• Candidate must hold a valid work permit or be eligible to work in the locations
• Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives. Applications
• Applications must include a CV, diploma(s) and a cover letter.

jobs.title: Stock exchange / Settlement Officer
jobs.description:
TEMPORARY MISSION - 9 MONTHS 
 
 Mission : 
 
• 

• Settlement of equities, bonds, structured products​ and investment funds transactions. 
 
 

• Main duties : 

• Monitoring of pending transactions. 

• Control and monitoring positions. 

• Contact with traders, custodians and counterparties. 

• Respect the process and its implementation in relation to operational requirements and volumes as directed by the bank. 
 
 

• Core competencies : 
 
 • Think in "Problem-Action-Results" terms 
 • Show strong client service ethic 
 • Be proactive and driven 
 • Express opinions and criticism in a constructive way 
 • Be a continuous learner 
 • Adopt change with a positive attitude 
 • Consistently comply with internal & external regulations 
 • Go beyond specific assignments, walk the extra mile 
 • Seek opportunities to improve the existing 
 • Be open to other's idea's 
 • Balance personal and team interests 
 • Seek opportunities to improve quality in all features of the work environment 
 • Maintain quality standards despite schedule pressures 

 
 
• Specific Competencies : 

• The complete flow of a transaction 

• The parties involved on the market (counterparties, custodians, clearing, companies) 

• Financial instruments (equities, bonds, structured products, investment funds) 

• Good management priorities and ability to deal with sensitive situations 

 
 
• Other Skills/Abilities : 

• CFC bank or equivalent 

• Ability to work within a team 

• French/English 

• MS Office / banking software / Swift 

• Knowledge of Omgeo and Euclid is a plus 

 
 
• Others : 
 
• Swiss Residence 
 

• Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Institutional Client Service Officer (M/F) - 12 Months Temporary Contract

titleByLocale.it: Responsabile del servizio clienti istituzionale (M/F) - Contratto temporaneo di 12 mesi
titleByLocale.en: Institutional Client Service Officer (M/F) - 12 Months Temporary Contract
titleByLocale.de: Institutional Client Service Officer (M/F) - 12 Months Temporary Contract
titleByLocale.fr: Institutional Client Service Officer (M/F) - 12 Months Temporary Contract

jobs.description:
Mission 
 Through the Asset Management division, we offer to institutional clients’ investment management capabilities organised across the business lines Long-Only Platform, Alternative Investment Solutions, Private Markets Group, Partnerships, Asset Allocation & DPM. 
 The Institutional Client Service Team serves as the primary contact for large and complex institutional clients globally, supporting sales teams in engaging institutional and wholesale clients. The team plays a vital role in enhancing profitability and driving growth within the Asset Management business. 
 We are seeking a dynamic candidate with a strong interest in Asset Management to join the Institutional Client Service team in Frankfurt . This role involves close collaboration with the German sales team, reporting locally to the Head of Wholesale EMEA and centrally to the COO - Institutional Clients & Head of the Client Service Team. The position covers activities with both institutional and wholesale clients in the DACH region (Germany, German-speaking Switzerland, and Austria) This position is for 12 months, but it may be renewed or offer other opportunities. 
 
 Main responsibilities 
 
• Assist Asset Management Sales teams in supporting German-speaking client-base. 

• Manage, execute, and follow up on client requests and inquiries. 

• Collaborate with internal teams throughout the client lifecycle to ensure client satisfaction and retention. 

• Coordinate the onboarding process and oversee the end-to-end transition. 

• Involve in the implementation and monitoring of the local sales strategy. 

• Support the team in their prospecting initiatives, including the preparation of marketing materials and conducting market research. 

• Contribute to the optimization of processes through the application of AI technologies. 

• Act as the local CRM champion, supporting and training the sales team to meet Salesforce requirements and ensure data accuracy. 

• Organise local events and roadshows. 

• Perform office management tasks. 

 
 Your Profile 
 
• Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Marketing, or a related field is beneficial. 

• Excellent organizational skills and capability to work independently. 

• Accuracy and attention to detail. 

• Advanced skills in standard IT applications (MS Office). 

• Mother tongue German and fluency in English is a “MUST”; French is a “nice to have”. 

 
 Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is a family-owned company, driven by an entrepreneurial vision, innovation and independence. UBP is one of the most strongly-capitalised private banks. 
 By joining UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. you will embrace a team of supportive high qualified professionals in Private Banking that will lead you to develop your career to the top level. We encourage continuous learning, development and knowledge sharing. We foster a culture of Agility, Conviction, Dedication and high degree of Responsibility. 
 UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A encourages all suitable qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity or expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. 
 In accordance with the provisions of the Luxembourg law of 23 July 2016 amending the law of 29 March 2013, retained candidate will be required to provide an extract from their criminal record as a part of UBP screening process.

• Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Marketing, or a related field is beneficial.
• Excellent organizational skills and capability to work independently.
• Accuracy and attention to detail.
• Advanced skills in standard IT applications (MS Office).
• Mother tongue German and fluency in English is a “MUST”; French is a “nice to have”. Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is a family-owned company, driven by an entrepreneurial vision, innovation and independence. UBP is one of the most strongly-capitalised private banks. By joining UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. you will embrace a team of supportive high qualified professionals in Private Banking that will lead you to develop your career to the top level. We encourage continuous learning, development and knowledge sharing. We foster a culture of Agility, Conviction, Dedication and high degree of Responsibility. UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A encourages all suitable qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity or expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. In accordance with the provisions of the Luxembourg law of 23 July 2016 amending the law of 29 March 2013, retained candidate will be required to provide an extract from their criminal record as a part of UBP screening process.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Mission Through the Asset Management division, we offer to institutional clients’ investment management capabilities organised across the business lines Long-Only Platform, Alternative Investment Solutions, Private Markets Group, Partnerships, Asset Allocation & DPM. The Institutional Client Service Team serves as the primary contact for large and complex institutional clients globally, supporting sales teams in engaging institutional and wholesale clients. The team plays a vital role in enhancing profitability and driving growth within the Asset Management business. We are seeking a dynamic candidate with a strong interest in Asset Management to join the Institutional Client Service team in Frankfurt . This role involves close collaboration with the German sales team, reporting locally to the Head of Wholesale EMEA and centrally to the COO - Institutional Clients & Head of the Client Service Team. The position covers activities with both institutional and wholesale clients in the DACH region (Germany, German-speaking Switzerland, and Austria) This position is for 12 months, but it may be renewed or offer other opportunities. Main responsibilities
• Assist Asset Management Sales teams in supporting German-speaking client-base.
• Manage, execute, and follow up on client requests and inquiries.
• Collaborate with internal teams throughout the client lifecycle to ensure client satisfaction and retention.
• Coordinate the onboarding process and oversee the end-to-end transition.
• Involve in the implementation and monitoring of the local sales strategy.
• Support the team in their prospecting initiatives, including the preparation of marketing materials and conducting market research.
• Contribute to the optimization of processes through the application of AI technologies.
• Act as the local CRM champion, supporting and training the sales team to meet Salesforce requirements and ensure data accuracy.
• Organise local events and roadshows.
• Perform office management tasks. Your Profile
• Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Marketing, or a related field is beneficial.

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Client Documentation Specialist
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 
 As part of the Client Documentation team, the Client Documentation Specialist is a key element to ensure:
 
• A smooth documents process flow between the different departments of the bank, especially regarding change in circumstances 

• The quality, coherence and the accuracy of the client's data in the bank's systems 

• The relevance of clients' instructions by performing control signature

• An efficient support to the other departments of the bank and the branches
 

• Main responsibilities 
 
 Day to day business 
 
 
• Review the documentation received from the clients for certain change in circumstances on an existing account or an account closing and verify that it complies with the local and international regulations (e.g. CDB20, Common Reporting Standards (CRS) and FATCA/QI rules) and the Bank's directives and procedures from (i) a formal point of view (e.g. the correct version of the document is signed, all the required fields have been completed) and (ii) that the information is consistent across the documents provided by the clients (IDs, corporate documents, bank's forms, etc…)

• Verify that the information entered in the Bank's systems by the Wealth Management is complete according to the documentation signed

• Perform 4 eyes controls and regular internal controls to ensure respect of the procedures 

• Ensure a good quality of service by providing accurate feedback to the Wealth Management on any error, missing document and answering in a timely manner to any query related to his/her mission and duties
 
 Coordination and central point of contact 
 
• Build a bridge and create synergy within the Client Documentation team and other key players (Legal, Compliance, IT…), create/maintain an efficient flow of communication that ensures all remain aware of important information and that it is consistent

• Act as a business partner by bringing his/her expertise and the relevant support and input to the other departments of the bank (Wealth Management, Compliance, Legal…) when required (on a complex situation, on the implementation of new regulations or bank's decisions, new projects)

• Coordinate efforts to help a smooth flow in the processes and always seek for improvements

• Facilitate problem solving and collaboration

• Create an environment oriented to trust, open communication and cohesive team effort
 
 Projects and Change management 
 
• Be up to date on any changes in the regulations, perform the assessment for internal changes, communicate with the relevant departments involved and to the team, implement them or launch projects, requests to IT as needed

• Participate in any project impacting the team, either required by a new regulation or initiated by the Bank, hence participate to workshops, meetings, upon the management delegation (for defining the business requirements, performing the testings, and any other required tasks)

• Involve the other team members as required

• Participate in the change management within the team by communicating proactively on the various projects and accompany the team in the change 

• Make sure that the procedures and processes are updated accordingly and communicated to all stakeholders, including the Client Documentation teams within the different branches.
 
 
 
• Your Profile 
 

• Education: Bachelors Degree

• Experience Technical skills: At ease with Microsoft tools, especially Excel

• Languages: English is a requirement, German/Italian or other language is a plus 

• Personal skills : Client service oriented, Curiosity and good interpersonal skills

• Swiss Residence mandatory 
 
 Core Competencies : 
 
• Open mind and team orientation: Be open to other's ideas. / Balance personal & team interests. / Express opinion and criticism in a constructive way.

• Innovation and development: Seek opportunities to improve the existing. / Be a continuous learner. / Adopt change with a positive attitude.

• Professional business culture: Think in 'Problem-Action-Results' terms. / Walk the extra mile. / Be proactive and driven. / Show strong client service ethic. / Build lasting client relationships.

• Quality, internal & external regulations: Consistently comply with internal & external regulations. / Seek opportunities to improve quality in all features of the work environment.

• Analytical Ability : Ability to assimilate procedures and to implement them. / Ability to identify issues and raise them in a structured way. / Ability to make process improvements. / Attention to detail.

• Solid organization and time management skills: Ability to organize the tasks efficiently based on priorities with anticipation of events. / Reaction and decision-making ability in face of unforeseen circumstances.

• Communication: Ability to raise issues via the right channel and in a constructive way. / Ability to expose constraints and opportunities. / Express opinions and criticism in a constructive way.

• Adaptability: Ability to change, be flexible. / Maintain quality standards despite schedule pressures.

• Team Spirit : Ability to proactively help the others. / Ability to finalize the common goal.
