jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Senior Legal Advisor - Management Company (M/F)
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 As a Senior Legal Advisor specialized in investment funds, you will play a central role within UBP Management Company, UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. You will be involved in a broad range of fund related legal and regulatory matters, including regulatory compliance, fund governance, and structuring of new products. 
 You will work closely with all stakeholders of the Management Company, providing legal expertise to support the company’s strategic and operational activities. You will also collaborate daily with our Product Management team and actively contributing to the structuring, management, and evolution of investment products. This key role will allow you to work on diverse projects and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams in a dynamic and international environment. 
 You will also be in charged of the corporate governance of the _Management Company and its branches. 
 
 
• Your main responsibilities will be: 
 
• Legal Advice : 
• Provide legal advice on the company’s activities, particularly in corporate law, investment fund law, and financial law. 

• Ensure the company’s activities comply with local and European regulations (CSSF, AIFMD, UCITS, etc.). 

• Draft, review, and negotiate contracts, partnership agreements, management mandates, and other legal documents. 
 

• Collaboration with the Product Management Team: 
• Work closely with the Product Management team on the structuring, implementation, and management of investment products. 

• Provide legal support for the development of new products and the update of existing ones. 
 

• Fund Management: 
• Oversee the creation, structuring, and legal management of investment funds (UCITS, AIF, SICAV, FIS, RAIF, etc.). 

• Prepare and update legal documentation for funds (prospectus, articles of association, management regulations, etc.). 

• Manage relationships with regulatory authorities (CSSF) and follow up on regulatory requests. 
 

• Compliance and Regulatory Monitoring : 
• Conduct legal and regulatory monitoring to anticipate legislative changes and their impact on the company’s activities. 

• Collaborate with the compliance team to ensure adherence to legal and regulatory obligations. 
 

• Litigation Management : 
• Handle potential disputes in collaboration with external law firms. 

• Defend the company’s interests in litigation or judicial proceedings. 
 

• Training and Support: 
• Train and raise awareness among internal teams about regulatory developments and best legal practices. 

• Provide legal support to various departments within the company. 
 
 
 
• Your Profile: 

• Master’s degree in Business Law, Corporate Law, or Financial Law. 

• Specialization in investment fund law or financial law is an asset. 

• 5 to 10 years of experience as a legal counsel in a Management Company, law firm, or financial institution in Luxembourg. 

• Proved experience in managing investment funds (UCITS, AIF, etc.). 

• Excellent knowledge of corporate law, investment fund law, and Luxembourg and European financial regulations (CSSF, AIFMD, UCITS, etc.). 

• Proficiency in the processes of structuring and managing investment funds. 

• Ability to draft and negotiate complex contracts in both French and English. 

• Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. 

• Excellent analytical and synthesis skills. 

• Ability to work independently while effectively collaborating with multidisciplinary teams. 

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

• Discretion and respect for confidentiality (knowledge of German or Luxembourgish is an asset). 
 
 Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is a family-owned company, driven by an entrepreneurial vision, innovation and independence. UBP is one of the most strongly-capitalised private banks. 
 By joining UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. you will embrace a team of supportive high qualified professionals in Private Banking that will lead you to develop your career to the top level. We encourage continuous learning, development and knowledge sharing. We foster a culture of Agility, Conviction, Dedication and high degree of Responsibility. 
 UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A encourages all suitable qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity or expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. 
 In accordance with the provisions of the Luxembourg law of 23 July 2016 amending the law of 29 March 2013, retained candidate will be required to provide an extract from their criminal record as a part of UBP screening process.

We encourage continuous learning, development and knowledge sharing. We foster a culture of Agility, Conviction, Dedication and high degree of Responsibility. UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A encourages all suitable qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity or expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. In accordance with the provisions of the Luxembourg law of 23 July 2016 amending the law of 29 March 2013, retained candidate will be required to provide an extract from their criminal record as a part of UBP screening process.
descriptionByLocale.en:
• Mission As a Senior Legal Advisor specialized in investment funds, you will play a central role within UBP Management Company, UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. You will be involved in a broad range of fund related legal and regulatory matters, including regulatory compliance, fund governance, and structuring of new products. You will work closely with all stakeholders of the Management Company, providing legal expertise to support the company’s strategic and operational activities. You will also collaborate daily with our Product Management team and actively contributing to the structuring, management, and evolution of investment products. This key role will allow you to work on diverse projects and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams in a dynamic and international environment. You will also be in charged of the corporate governance of the _Management Company and its branches.
• Your main responsibilities will be:
• Legal Advice :
• Provide legal advice on the company’s activities, particularly in corporate law, investment fund law, and financial law.
• Ensure the company’s activities comply with local and European regulations (CSSF, AIFMD, UCITS, etc.).
• Draft, review, and negotiate contracts, partnership agreements, management mandates, and other legal documents.
• Collaboration with the Product Management Team:
• Work closely with the Product Management team on the structuring, implementation, and management of investment products.
• Provide legal support for the development of new products and the update of existing ones.
• Fund Management:
• Oversee the creation, structuring, and legal management of investment funds (UCITS, AIF, SICAV, FIS, RAIF, etc.).
• Prepare and update legal documentation for funds (prospectus, articles of association, management regulations, etc.).
• Manage relationships with regulatory authorities (CSSF) and follow up on regulatory requests.
• Compliance and Regulatory Monitoring :
• Conduct legal and regulatory monitoring to anticipate legislative changes and their impact on the company’s activities.
• Collaborate with the compliance team to ensure adherence to legal and regulatory obligations.
• Litigation Management :
• Handle potential disputes in collaboration with external law firms.
• Defend the company’s interests in litigation or judicial proceedings.
• Training and Support:
• Train and raise awareness among internal teams about regulatory developments and best legal practices.
• Provide legal support to various departments within the company.
• Your Profile:
• Master’s degree in Business Law, Corporate Law, or Financial Law.
• Specialization in investment fund law or financial law is an asset.
• 5 to 10 years of experience as a legal counsel in a Management Company, law firm, or financial institution in Luxembourg.
• Proved experience in managing investment funds (UCITS, AIF, etc.).
• Excellent knowledge of corporate law, investment fund law, and Luxembourg and European financial regulations (CSSF, AIFMD, UCITS, etc.).
• Proficiency in the processes of structuring and managing investment funds.
• Ability to draft and negotiate complex contracts in both French and English.
• Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
• Excellent analytical and synthesis skills.
• Ability to work independently while effectively collaborating with multidisciplinary teams.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
• Discretion and respect for confidentiality (knowledge of German or Luxembourgish is an asset). Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is a family-owned company, driven by an entrepreneurial vision, innovation and independence. UBP is one of the most strongly-capitalised private banks. By joining UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. you will embrace a team of supportive high qualified professionals in Private Banking that will lead you to develop your career to the top level. We encourage continuous learning, development and knowledge sharing. We foster a culture of Agility, Conviction, Dedication and high degree of Responsibility. UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A encourages all suitable qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity or expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. In accordance with the provisions of the Luxembourg law of 23 July 2016 amending the law of 29 March 2013, retained candidate will be required to provide an extract from their criminal record as a part of UBP screening process.

jobs.title: Graduate Programme Jersey, 2026, Wealth Management

titleByLocale.it: Programma di laurea Jersey, 2026, Gestione della ricchezza

jobs.description:
Job Description - Please note we do not offer sponsorship
 
This is a unique 12-month programme based on a permanent contract within our Wealth Management Team in Jersey. We are offering an exceptional opportunity for a recent master’s graduate to join our exclusive programme, designed to provide in-depth exposure to Wealth Management at a renowned Private Bank.
The programme offers a comprehensive learning experience, enabling you to gain valuable insights into the finance industry and develop essential skills for a successful career in private banking.
 
The graduate programme consists of 3 rotations each lasting 4 months. These rotations take place in different teams, all closely connected to Wealth Management and Treasury and Trading, providing an excellent opportunity to build a broad internal network of colleagues and experts. Furthermore, you will benefit from shadowing experiences, gaining valuable exposure to various aspects of the bank’s operation.
This programme is specifically designed for young graduates holding a master’s degree, with a maximum of 12 months of professional experience since graduation. You will have the opportunity to transition into a permanent role within the team after completing the programme.
 
Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) – Wealth Management:
Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is one of the largest family-owned private banks in the world. But what makes UBP unique is its corporate culture, which combines modernity and tradition. Although UBP was founded in 1969, its origins can be traced back to the 17th century and the start of a long line of financiers and diplomats of whom Edgar de Picciotto and his descendants are the successors. Today, the Bank's staff draw their inspiration and drive from the founding family's subtle blend of strong moral values and pragmatism, which to this day remain the key to UBP’s success.
 
As it plays such a major part in our strategy, we are investing significantly in Wealth Management to fuel our growth, particularly in Europe, the Middle East, Eastern Europe, Latin America, and Asia. We provide a complete range of private banking products and services, including a variety of investment management and advisory mandates, tailored to our clients’ specific requirements, objectives, and risk profiles. Among UBP professional partners, over 300 independent asset managers have chosen us as their custodian bank, valuing our personalised, flexible services and our transparent fee structure.
 
 Role Description:
 
• You will be part of the Wealth Management Team in Jersey:
• Relationship with private clients and marketing
• With the guidance of relationship managers and experts:

Cultivate relationships with clients

Respond to clients’/prospects’ needs with investments proposals

Ensure that contractual documents and supporting paperwork are signed and up to date

Comply with the Bank’s internal directives, considering the client’s profile and types of transactions
Assisting relationship managers With the guidance of relationship managers and experts:

Follow up on and execute administrative tasks: account paperwork filing, transaction executions, trade executions, transfers, discretionary management and advisory mandate processing, client query responses

Ensure clients’ transactions/instructions/requests (stock market orders, transfers, tax report requests, product information, etc.) are executed and addressed correctly and swiftly

Provide administrative support to the relationship managers by preparing visits and trips, filing client data and documents, and checking that clients’ investments are consistent with their risk profiles
UNION BANCAIRE PRIVÉE, UBP SA | WM GRADUATE PROGRAMME | MARCH26 2 | 3

• Carry out first-level checks as per internal directives, where applicable, and escalate transactions or situations putting the Bank at risk, to the line manager
• Treasury and Trading
• With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager

Forecast near and long-term cash follows across the entity

Maintain buffers and contingency liquidity

• Coordinate intercompany funding and optimise notional/physical pooling
• Funding and Capital Markets
• With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager

Plan and execute short- and long-term funding, i.e. bonds and loans

Maintain bank and investor relationships

• Monitor costs of funds and refinance risk, manage maturities
• Risk Management
• With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager

Identify exposures

Set and enforce risk limits, hedging and effective metrics

Execute hedges via forward, swaps and options

Stress testing, sensitivity analysis and scenario planning
Compliance with regulations and internal directives

Learn to know and strictly apply all internal directives and rules, baking laws and regulations, such as those regarding entering business relations with clients, contacts/relations with clients (existing or prospective), anti-money-laundering, cross-border business, investment policies, investor protection, business travel

Attend the Bank's compulsory training session
Requirements:

Educational Background: Master’s degree in financial economics recently obtained from a highly recognised university

Work Experience: Relevant initial work experience in the finance industry (internships), preferably within Private Banking or Wealth Management

Technical Skills: Strong knowledge of Excel

Language Proficiency: Fluency in English is required

Key Skills: Strong analytical skills, structured approach, excellent communication, and a proactive, adaptable mindset. Ability to collaborate across business and technical teams, with a self-motivated, results-driven attitude

Work Authorisation: Applicants must have the legal right to work in the UK and Jersey at the time of application. We do not offer sponsorship

Jersey residential and employment status
Applications Process: We only accept online applications submitted through our official website, ensure that your application includes the following documents:

CV

Motivation Letter

Grade Transcript & Diplomas

Reference Letters
• UNION BANCAIRE PRIVÉE, UBP SA | WM GRADUATE PROGRAMME | MARCH26 3 | 3
• Key Dates: ¦ Programme start date: 1st September 2026 ¦ Duration of programme: 12 months (permanent contract) ¦ Interviews Period: March to June 2026

Ability to collaborate across business and technical teams, with a self-motivated, results-driven attitude Work Authorisation: Applicants must have the legal right to work in the UK and Jersey at the time of application. We do not offer sponsorship Jersey residential and employment status Applications Process: We only accept online applications submitted through our official website, ensure that your application includes the following documents: CV Motivation Letter Grade Transcript & Diplomas Reference Letters UNION BANCAIRE PRIVÉE, UBP SA | WM GRADUATE PROGRAMME | MARCH26 3 | 3 Key Dates: ¦ Programme start date: 1st September 2026 ¦ Duration of programme: 12 months (permanent contract) ¦ Interviews Period: March to June 2026
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Job Description - Please note we do not offer sponsorship This is a unique 12-month programme based on a permanent contract within our Wealth Management Team in Jersey. We are offering an exceptional opportunity for a recent master’s graduate to join our exclusive programme, designed to provide in-depth exposure to Wealth Management at a renowned Private Bank. The programme offers a comprehensive learning experience, enabling you to gain valuable insights into the finance industry and develop essential skills for a successful career in private banking. The graduate programme consists of 3 rotations each lasting 4 months. These rotations take place in different teams, all closely connected to Wealth Management and Treasury and Trading, providing an excellent opportunity to build a broad internal network of colleagues and experts. Furthermore, you will benefit from shadowing experiences, gaining valuable exposure to various aspects of the bank’s operation. This programme is specifically designed for young graduates holding a master’s degree, with a maximum of 12 months of professional experience since graduation. You will have the opportunity to transition into a permanent role within the team after completing the programme. Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) – Wealth Management: Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is one of the largest family-owned private banks in the world. But what makes UBP unique is its corporate culture, which combines modernity and tradition. Although UBP was founded in 1969, its origins can be traced back to the 17th century and the start of a long line of financiers and diplomats of whom Edgar de Picciotto and his descendants are the successors. Today, the Bank's staff draw their inspiration and drive from the founding family's subtle blend of strong moral values and pragmatism, which to this day remain the key to UBP’s success. As it plays such a major part in our strategy, we are investing significantly in Wealth Management to fuel our growth, particularly in Europe, the Middle East, Eastern Europe, Latin America, and Asia. We provide a complete range of private banking products and services, including a variety of investment management and advisory mandates, tailored to our clients’ specific requirements, objectives, and risk profiles. Among UBP professional partners, over 300 independent asset managers have chosen us as their custodian bank, valuing our personalised, flexible services and our transparent fee structure. Role Description: You will be part of the Wealth Management Team in Jersey: Relationship with private clients and marketing With the guidance of relationship managers and experts: Cultivate relationships with clients Respond to clients’/prospects’ needs with investments proposals Ensure that contractual documents and supporting paperwork are signed and up to date Comply with the Bank’s internal directives, considering the client’s profile and types of transactions Assisting relationship managers With the guidance of relationship managers and experts: Follow up on and execute administrative tasks: account paperwork filing, transaction executions, trade executions, transfers, discretionary management and advisory mandate processing, client query responses Ensure clients’ transactions/instructions/requests (stock market orders, transfers, tax report requests, product information, etc.) are executed and addressed correctly and swiftly Provide administrative support to the relationship managers by preparing visits and trips, filing client data and documents, and checking that clients’ investments are consistent with their risk profiles UNION BANCAIRE PRIVÉE, UBP SA | WM GRADUATE PROGRAMME | MARCH26 2 | 3 Carry out first-level checks as per internal directives, where applicable, and escalate transactions or situations putting the Bank at risk, to the line manager Treasury and Trading With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager Forecast near and long-term cash follows across the entity Maintain buffers and contingency liquidity Coordinate intercompany funding and optimise notional/physical pooling Funding and Capital Markets With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager Plan and execute short- and long-term funding, i.e. bonds and loans Maintain bank and investor relationships Monitor costs of funds and refinance risk, manage maturities Risk Management With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager Identify exposures Set and enforce risk limits, hedging and effective metrics Execute hedges via forward, swaps and options Stress testing, sensitivity analysis and scenario planning Compliance with regulations and internal directives Learn to know and strictly apply all internal directives and rules, baking laws and regulations, such as those regarding entering business relations with clients, contacts/relations with clients (existing or prospective), anti-money-laundering, cross-border business, investment policies, investor protection, business travel Attend the Bank's compulsory training session

jobs.title: Accounts Payable Officer
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 The Invoice Officer in Accounts Payable is responsible for the accurate processing of invoices and ensuring timely payments to vendors (approximately 350 invoices per month). 
 This role involves maintaining financial records, resolving discrepancies, and supporting the overall account payable function. 
 The Invoice Officer will also collaborate closely with IT procurement and business IT partners to enhance operational efficiency and ensure alignment with financial practices. 
 
 
• Main responsibilities include but not limited to : 
 
 1. Invoice Processing: 
 - Review and verify invoices for accuracy and completeness. 
 - Enter invoices into the information system (Coupa/Dynamics Navision) and ensure proper coding. 
 - Participate in projects focused on processes transformation 
 
 2. Payment Processing: 
 - Prepare and process payment runs. 
 - Monitor payment schedules and ensure timely payments to vendors. 
 
 3. Vendor Management: 
 - Communicate with vendors regarding invoice status and payment inquiries. 
 - Assist in resolving any disputes or discrepancies. 
 
 4. Record Keeping: 
 - Maintain accurate and organized accounts payable records. 
 - Prepare and manage monthly accruals to ensure accurate financial reporting and compliance with accounting standards. 
 - Provide detailed business allocation of depreciation for projects 
 - Ensure all invoices are filed according to company policy. 
 
 5. Reporting: 
 - Assist in generating accounts payable reports for management review. 
 - Support month-end closing activities related to accounts payable. 
 - Provide detailed report related to mobile communication costs on a quarterly basis. 
 
 6. Compliance: 
 - Ensure compliance with company policies and accounting standards. 
 - Assist in audits by providing necessary documentation and information. 
 
 
• Qualifications: 
 

• Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field preferred. 

• Previous experience in accounts payable or a similar role is mandatory. 

• Previous experience with Coupa and MS Dynamics is preferable 

• Proficient in accounting software and Microsoft Office Suite. 

• Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in data entry and financial reporting. 

• Proficient verbal and written communication skills in both French and English for effective collaboration with vendors and internal teams. 

• Ability to work independently and as part of a team. 
 
 
• Others 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Process Transformation Officer
jobs.description:
Mission: 
 Support the continuous improvement of Financial Crime Compliance (FCC) processes (AML, KYC, Sanctions) by working closely with Compliance, Business, and IT teams. Contribute to aligning Compliance requirements with operational processes and IT tools while enhancing process efficiency and establishing a robust control framework. 
 Responsibilities 
 
• Support the analysis of business needs and contribute to integrating compliance requirements into financial crime prevention processes (AML, KYC, Sanctions). Assist in identifying process improvement opportunities and implementing solutions validated by stakeholders (Compliance, Front Office, IT, etc.). 

• Design and implement streamlined, scalable, and effective processes to enhance operational performance and compliance with regulatory requirements. 

• Participate in the documentation and updating of processes related to compliance, including Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), ensuring alignment with current practices and regulatory requirements. 

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams (e.g. Tools & Projects teams, etc.) for the development, maintenance, and testing of compliance applications (IMTF, smartKYC, Pythagoras, etc.). 

• Contribute to change management , particularly by supporting training and communication initiatives for users. 

• Participate in functional testing of implemented solutions, using provided methodologies and tools, and ensuring rigorous follow-up of results. 

• Support the improvement of control frameworks related to compliance tools, including monitoring incidents, analyzing data quality, and collecting user feedback. 

• Assist in monitoring compliance tools , ensuring the proper functioning of data flows, updating parameters, and conducting regular review exercises in collaboration with IT and Tools & Projects teams. 

• Contribute to project management related to AML, KYC, and sanctions by participating in local or group initiatives and engaging in project management methodologies (Agile, Waterfall). 

• Participate in technological watch and identify opportunities to improve compliance tools and processes. 

• Support the follow-up of audit recommendations related to operational compliance processes, ensuring their implementation within the required deadlines. 

 
 Your Profil 
 
• Education and experience: experience in compliance financial crime, risk management, or operational processes is preferred. At least 5 years of experience and experience in a consulting firm (Big 4 ou equivalent) is an asset 

• Analytical skills: Ability to analyze processes, identify ineficciencies, gaps and areas for improvement, and propose appropriate solutions. 

• Communication skills: Proficiency in drafting clear and structured documents, as well as communicating effectively with various stakeholders. 

• Team spirit: Ability to collaborate effectively with multidisciplinary teams (Compliance, Business, IT) and adapt to different interlocutors. 

• Organization and rigor: Ability to manage multiple tasks and projects simultaneously while respecting deadlines and priorities. 

• Technical knowledge: familiarity with compliance technology solutions, data analytics tools, and automation platforms. Experience or interest in data management, reporting, or process modeling tools is a plus. 

• Languages: Proficiency in French and English, both written and spoken. 

• Swiss residence: Mandatory 

 Core competencies 
 
• Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility, and Responsibility. 

• Compliance with regulations and internal directives 

• Curiosity and willingness to learn in a constantly evolving environment. 

 Why Join Our Team? 
 You will join a dynamic and collaborative team where you will have the opportunity to develop your skills in compliance and project management while benefiting from guidance and support to advance your career. You will be involved in strategic and innovative projects at the heart of FCC department. 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Phone Operator
jobs.description:
• Mission 
• Manage a high-volume, high-standard French/English switchboard (additional languages are an asset), administer the VOIP system (AGILE), and handle CFF travel arrangements.

• Join a private bank and interact with a wide range of stakeholders: various departments, VIPs, and senior executives.

 

• Main responsibilities 
 
 
• CISCO VOIP system management: 
 
 
• Handle external and internal calls with strict confidentiality and exceptional courtesy.

• Rigorously enforce security standards, particularly regarding phishing attempts and malicious calls.

• Manage call transfers and message taking using the Samwin operator console. 

• Cover Executive Management calls during assistants’ breaks and absences, as well as for certain management groups.

 

• CFF travel management: 
 
• Provide support and advice for domestic CFF travel. 

• Process requests for international travel. 

• Manage the renewal of CFF subscriptions.

 

• Ticketing and facilities requests: 
 
• Administer the ticketing system for facility management requests (e.g., ventilation). 

• Coordinate with relevant stakeholders, ensure follow-up, and close action items.

 
 

• Your Profile 

• Experience: 
 
 At least 5 years of experience in a similar role within a highly demanding environment is required Technical skills 
 

• Languages: 
• French and English must be mastered perfectly (minimum C1 level). 

• Any additional language is an asset. 
 

• Personal skills: 
• Motivated, dynamic, proactive, and compassionate professional with a service mindset. 

• Natural aptitude for pedagogy, able to make technical topics accessible and easy to understand. 

• Active listening skills with both internal and external stakeholders. 

• Strong stress management, maintaining composure in all circumstances. 

• Discreet and tactful, exercising restraint and professionalism at all times. 

• Team-oriented, committed to sharing knowledge, fostering collective intelligence, and learning from the surrounding experience. 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Funds Execution Officer
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 Ensure the accurate and timely execution of client fund orders across Mutual Funds, Hedge Funds, and Private Equity Funds for all UBP branches, while maintaining strict compliance with internal processes and regulatory requirements. Deliver exceptional service to clients to ensure seamless end-to-end fund order processing.
 
• Main responsibilities 
 
 Execute client fund orders across Mutual Funds, Hedge Funds, Private Equity. Mutual funds are processed primarily via SWIFT through a global custodian while Alternatives (HF/PE) are placed directly with fund administrators, including completion of required subscription/redemption documentation and KYC/AML. Coordinate funding, settlement, and confirmations; manage pre-funding, notice periods, and specific terms (gates/lock-ups).
 
• Your Profile 
 
 
• Education and Technical Skills 

• Bachelor’s degree in Economics (or equivalent). 

• Proficient in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, Word, Outlook, VBA a plus). 

• Familiarity with fund order workflows and transfer agent processes environments is an asset
 

• Personal Skills 

• Think in “Problem–Action–Results” terms; proactive and driven. 

• Strong client service ethic; clear and professional communicator. 

• Highly organized with excellent attention to detail; able to manage tight deadlines and work under pressure in a high-volume setting. 

• Strong analytical skills with the ability to identify issues, propose solutions, and follow through to completion. 

• Constructive, collaborative, and committed to continuous learning. 

• Adaptable, self-motivated, and a team player. 

• Sound understanding of operational risks in fund execution with disciplined control and timely escalation. 
 
 
• Languages 

• French and English: fluent (written and spoken).
 
 
• Others 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Investor Protection
jobs.description:
Main Responsibilities 
 The UK Regulatory Compliance Team is primarily responsible for providing timely, risk-based advice, challenge, and enablement to the business on regulatory matters. The Investor Protection Compliance team sits within UK Regulatory Compliance and assists in managing compliance risk across UBP UK, contributing to regulatory change implementation, and helping to maintain effective policies, training, and governance in line with UK requirements and UBP standards relating to investor and client protection. Investor Protection Compliance ensure investors and clients are treated fairly and transparently, topics include but are not limited to; disclosures, suitability & appropriateness, conflicts of interest, complaints handling, marketing and client communications, vulnerable customers, responsible lending, product governance and best execution. 
 
 
• The role holder is responsible for: 
 
• supporting the team in fulfilling the responsibilities set out above. 

• supporting the team and assisting in the management of compliance risk across UBP UK’s business. In providing advisory support, the role holder will liaise with colleagues in the UK Regulatory Compliance team, Financial Crime and overseas offices as appropriate to ensure local regulations and wider department processes are considered. 

• providing advice and enabling support and independent challenge to any areas of the UBP business or any topics as agreed with management. The role holder will also be expected to support business projects and provide cover for other members of the team as required. 

• coordinating team activities, including but not limited to managing the mailbox and query management, new product and service reviews, product and service ongoing reviews and financial promotions. 

• reviewing financial promotions, marketing materials and client facing communications. 

• initial reviews and coordinating the update of relevant policies and processes considering UK regulatory requirements and market and business development 

• assisting with the analysis of regulatory issues arising and implementation of relevant projects and regulatory changes 

• working with and supporting the team, to respond to advisory queries, upgrade and implement policies considering regulatory developments, and contribute to risk assessments, training and internal governance reporting activity as required. 

 
 Role Duties 
 Investor Protection Compliance support and coordination: 
 
• Assist in monitoring external regulatory and business developments and help develop and implement policies and procedures to reflect new requirements 

• Support in analysis of regulatory developments where required for investor protection topics 

• Assist in analysing and preparing for the impact of upcoming regulatory developments 

• Contribute to compliance reporting, dashboards, and management updates, ensuring data is timely, accurate, and consistent. 

• Contribute to improving the process and procedures to meet consumer duty principles and other conduct requirements within the regulatory framework, Treating Customers Fairly and sound risk management 

• Provide stakeholders with sound and timely advice, guidance and support, where appropriate. 

• Assist in the Regular review of Investor Protection related Policies and other written guidance. 

• Assist in evaluating and progressing proposed new products/ services and other business initiatives. 

• Assist in developing and maintaining training, communications, and guidance that translates regulatory expectations into practical steps for the business. 

• Initial review and investigation, where required, of escalations from business assurance relating to; cross border, client-opt ups, suitability and appropriateness. 

• Review of regular reports related to complaints from business assurance 

• Help to identify regulatory compliance risks. 

 Team Management and Collaboration: 
 
• Uphold the UBP values and, by example, support culture of compliance with policies, appropriate risk management and the principle of treating customers fairly. 

• Support management and the team through active participation in regular team and 1-2-1 meetings 

• Partner with business stakeholders to support compliance objectives and business enablement 

• Participate in group projects as appropriate and required 

• Work within the team to help identify areas for improvement and efficiency 

• Support the Compliance team and act as cover for other members where required. 

 Business & Market Knowledge 
 
• Develop and sustain technical knowledge across the products and services of UBP to ensure business is supported. 

• Retain updated personal knowledge of wealth management/private banking market, ensuring that the reputation of UBP is upheld to the highest level possible in terms of compliance and reputation internally and externally and to consider treating customers fairly and good customer outcomes. 

 Internal Control 
 
• Maintain full, accurate files/notes of key meetings and advice using UBP core systems to store. 

• Help log, triage, and track compliance issues, incidents, and inquiries 

• Support root-cause analysis and follow-up actions under guidance from senior team members. 

 The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. Specific duties may evolve based on business needs, regulatory changes, and the candidate’s strengths and interests. 
 
 Committee Membership 
 
• None 
 
 Conduct Rules 
 This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 
• To act with integrity; 

• To act with due skill, care and diligence; 

• To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; 

• To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; 

• To observe proper standards of market conduct; and 

• To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. 
 
 Person Specification 
 Knowledge, Skills & Experience 
 
 
• Good understanding of UK financial regulations including but not limited to: MiFID II, Consumer Duty, FCA rulebook 

• Compliance qualifications (ICA or CISI) desirable 

• 2 years+ Financial Services experience preferable 

• Wealth management experience is desirable 

• Ability to assess and analyse information 

• Ability to interpret regulation, identify risks and analyse potential control gaps 

• Strong attention to detail and focus 

• Good written & communication skills 

• Numeracy, drafting and record keeping 

• Team Spirit & Collaboration 

• Attention to detail and focus 

• Good project / change management skills

• Retain updated personal knowledge of wealth management/private banking market, ensuring that the reputation of UBP is upheld to the highest level possible in terms of compliance and reputation internally and externally and to consider treating customers fairly and good customer outcomes. Internal Control
• Maintain full, accurate files/notes of key meetings and advice using UBP core systems to store.
• Help log, triage, and track compliance issues, incidents, and inquiries
• Support root-cause analysis and follow-up actions under guidance from senior team members. The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. Specific duties may evolve based on business needs, regulatory changes, and the candidate’s strengths and interests. Committee Membership
• None Conduct Rules This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are:
• To act with integrity;
• To act with due skill, care and diligence;
• To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators;
• To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly;
• To observe proper standards of market conduct; and
• To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. Person Specification Knowledge, Skills & Experience
• Good understanding of UK financial regulations including but not limited to: MiFID II, Consumer Duty, FCA rulebook
• Compliance qualifications (ICA or CISI) desirable
• 2 years+ Financial Services experience preferable
• Wealth management experience is desirable
• Ability to assess and analyse information
• Ability to interpret regulation, identify risks and analyse potential control gaps
• Strong attention to detail and focus
• Good written & communication skills
• Numeracy, drafting and record keeping
• Team Spirit & Collaboration
• Attention to detail and focus
• Good project / change management skills
descriptionByLocale.de:
Main Responsibilities The UK Regulatory Compliance Team is primarily responsible for providing timely, risk-based advice, challenge, and enablement to the business on regulatory matters. The Investor Protection Compliance team sits within UK Regulatory Compliance and assists in managing compliance risk across UBP UK, contributing to regulatory change implementation, and helping to maintain effective policies, training, and governance in line with UK requirements and UBP standards relating to investor and client protection. Investor Protection Compliance ensure investors and clients are treated fairly and transparently, topics include but are not limited to; disclosures, suitability & appropriateness, conflicts of interest, complaints handling, marketing and client communications, vulnerable customers, responsible lending, product governance and best execution.
• The role holder is responsible for:
• supporting the team in fulfilling the responsibilities set out above.
• supporting the team and assisting in the management of compliance risk across UBP UK’s business. In providing advisory support, the role holder will liaise with colleagues in the UK Regulatory Compliance team, Financial Crime and overseas offices as appropriate to ensure local regulations and wider department processes are considered.
• providing advice and enabling support and independent challenge to any areas of the UBP business or any topics as agreed with management. The role holder will also be expected to support business projects and provide cover for other members of the team as required.
• coordinating team activities, including but not limited to managing the mailbox and query management, new product and service reviews, product and service ongoing reviews and financial promotions.
• reviewing financial promotions, marketing materials and client facing communications.
• initial reviews and coordinating the update of relevant policies and processes considering UK regulatory requirements and market and business development
• assisting with the analysis of regulatory issues arising and implementation of relevant projects and regulatory changes
• working with and supporting the team, to respond to advisory queries, upgrade and implement policies considering regulatory developments, and contribute to risk assessments, training and internal governance reporting activity as required. Role Duties Investor Protection Compliance support and coordination:
• Assist in monitoring external regulatory and business developments and help develop and implement policies and procedures to reflect new requirements
• Support in analysis of regulatory developments where required for investor protection topics
• Assist in analysing and preparing for the impact of upcoming regulatory developments
• Contribute to compliance reporting, dashboards, and management updates, ensuring data is timely, accurate, and consistent.
• Contribute to improving the process and procedures to meet consumer duty principles and other conduct requirements within the regulatory framework, Treating Customers Fairly and sound risk management
• Provide stakeholders with sound and timely advice, guidance and support, where appropriate.
• Assist in the Regular review of Investor Protection related Policies and other written guidance.
• Assist in evaluating and progressing proposed new products/ services and other business initiatives.
• Assist in developing and maintaining training, communications, and guidance that translates regulatory expectations into practical steps for the business.
• Initial review and investigation, where required, of escalations from business assurance relating to; cross border, client-opt ups, suitability and appropriateness.
• Review of regular reports related to complaints from business assurance
• Help to identify regulatory compliance risks. Team Management and Collaboration:
• Uphold the UBP values and, by example, support culture of compliance with policies, appropriate risk management and the principle of treating customers fairly.
• Support management and the team through active participation in regular team and 1-2-1 meetings
• Partner with business stakeholders to support compliance objectives and business enablement
• Participate in group projects as appropriate and required
• Work within the team to help identify areas for improvement and efficiency
• Support the Compliance team and act as cover for other members where required. Business & Market Knowledge
• Develop and sustain technical knowledge across the products and services of UBP to ensure business is supported.
• Retain updated personal knowledge of wealth management/private banking market, ensuring that the reputation of UBP is upheld to the highest level possible in terms of compliance and reputation internally and externally and to consider treating customers fairly and good customer outcomes. Internal Control
• Maintain full, accurate files/notes of key meetings and advice using UBP core systems to store.
• Help log, triage, and track compliance issues, incidents, and inquiries
• Support root-cause analysis and follow-up actions under guidance from senior team members. The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. Specific duties may evolve based on business needs, regulatory changes, and the candidate’s strengths and interests. Committee Membership
• None Conduct Rules This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are:
• To act with integrity;
• To act with due skill, care and diligence;
• To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators;
• To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly;
• To observe proper standards of market conduct; and
• To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. Person Specification Knowledge, Skills & Experience
• Good understanding of UK financial regulations including but not limited to: MiFID II, Consumer Duty, FCA rulebook
• Compliance qualifications (ICA or CISI) desirable
• 2 years+ Financial Services experience preferable
• Wealth management experience is desirable
• Ability to assess and analyse information
• Ability to interpret regulation, identify risks and analyse potential control gaps
• Strong attention to detail and focus
• Good written & communication skills
• Numeracy, drafting and record keeping
• Team Spirit & Collaboration
• Attention to detail and focus
• Good project / change management skills

jobs.title: Full-Stack Developer - Wealth Management
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 The candidate will be part of the Data Intelligence & Digitalisation team within Wealth Management and will take part in the development of the digital platform for private bankers. The Wealth Management is currently enhancing its digital platform with the objectives to better integrate data and services for bankers and clients. The candidate will have to work in a complex environment involving technical and business challenges with many stakeholders. 
 
• Main responsibilities 
 
 
• Participate in the development effort of the digital platform 

• By integrating existing web services into a new user experience 

• Develop screens and navigation in the intranet Portal 

• Design and implement new features across the existing software stack 

• Define and challenge upcoming features from a technical perspective 

• Navigate through a large set of existing databases and write optimized queries against them 

• Work closely with business and quantitative analysts to ensure that technical solutions are feasible and meet the needs of the business 
 
 
• Your Profile 
 
 
• 3 to 5 years experience

• Excellent computing and analytical skills. In particular, the ability to drill-down in existing code in the absence of any documentation 

• Proactive and user-focused, with a genuine interest in the functional as well as technical aspects of each challenge encountered 

• Full-stack development across .NET 8.0 (C#) and legacy .NET Framework applications, plus JavaScript (Vue.js) 

• Knowledge of web-based application development. Interest in UI/UX. Knowledge of PrimeVue and AG grid will be a plus 

• Broad experience of databases. SQL (Oracle) will be used as well as Entity Framework 

• Knowledge of continuous integration/continuous deployment (ideally with Azure Devops, Ansible, Jenkins and GIT) 

• The ability to adapt to and learn new skills and techniques in a very dynamic and changing environment 
 
 Nice to have 
 
• Worked in an Agile development environment with Jira 

• Knowledge of finance and banking is a plus 
 
 
• Others 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Market Specialist Emerging Markets
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 Ensuring that new and/or existing clients to the bank, for the Emerging Markets & Eastern Europe region, are compliant with laws, regulations such as AML, CDB, FATCA, CRS and internal guidelines and standards. To provide market expertise and support to the Compliance WM team, handling the day-to-day spectrum of account openings and mutations for Geneva, ensure that business lines adhere to applicable regulatory requirements (Finma, CDB, etc…).
 
 
• Main responsibilities 
• Analyse all kind of Compliance High Risk and complex cases with the Private Banking activity

• Review account openings and perform checks in databases and searches to identify and mitigate any AML, legal or reputational risk

• Prepare memos and submit cases to relevant Committee/s of the Bank 

• Monitor law and regulatory changes which may lead to change in customer servicing and internal policies, both within the core Market as well as in Switzerland

• Assure that local and Swiss laws, regulations, and internal guidelines are respected in the process of the account opening and modifications

• May be involved in different Compliance Projects

• Act as business partner to other team members and departments of the Bank when required

• Any other task required by Management relating to the Mission of the employee

• Work collaboratively with other Compliance teams within and outside Switzerland

• Provide guidance, Market expertise, support and relevant information to the Front Office
 
 
 
• Your Profile 
• Previous experience of at least 5 years in a similar function with market expertise in the Emerging Markets and Eastern Europe region

• University degree or completed by the Certificate of Compliance Management or equivalent

• Proficiency level of French and English.

• Proficiency level in Russian

• Capacity to manage many tasks at the same time

• Interpersonal skills, very good in negotiation 

• Strong team spirit

• Synthetic and pragmatic, with a good analytical spirit

• Delivery oriented

• Discreet
 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

• Mission Ensuring that new and/or existing clients to the bank, for the Emerging Markets & Eastern Europe region, are compliant with laws, regulations such as AML, CDB, FATCA, CRS and internal guidelines and standards. To provide market expertise and support to the Compliance WM team, handling the day-to-day spectrum of account openings and mutations for Geneva, ensure that business lines adhere to applicable regulatory requirements (Finma, CDB, etc…).
• Main responsibilities
• Analyse all kind of Compliance High Risk and complex cases with the Private Banking activity
• Review account openings and perform checks in databases and searches to identify and mitigate any AML, legal or reputational risk
• Prepare memos and submit cases to relevant Committee/s of the Bank
• Monitor law and regulatory changes which may lead to change in customer servicing and internal policies, both within the core Market as well as in Switzerland
• Assure that local and Swiss laws, regulations, and internal guidelines are respected in the process of the account opening and modifications
• May be involved in different Compliance Projects
• Act as business partner to other team members and departments of the Bank when required
• Any other task required by Management relating to the Mission of the employee
• Work collaboratively with other Compliance teams within and outside Switzerland
• Provide guidance, Market expertise, support and relevant information to the Front Office
• Your Profile
• Previous experience of at least 5 years in a similar function with market expertise in the Emerging Markets and Eastern Europe region
• University degree or completed by the Certificate of Compliance Management or equivalent
• Proficiency level of French and English.
• Proficiency level in Russian
• Capacity to manage many tasks at the same time
• Interpersonal skills, very good in negotiation
• Strong team spirit
• Synthetic and pragmatic, with a good analytical spirit
• Delivery oriented
• Discreet Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Investments Data Analyst - Temporary
jobs.description:
Investments Data Analyst - Portfolio Guardian 
 12 months temporary mission 
 About COO Investment Management: 
 
• The COO Investment Management department at Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is the operational backbone of the bank’s investment platform. It orchestrates the strategy, governance, processes, data, and technology that enable UBP’s investment teams to deliver high-quality, compliant, and scalable outcomes for clients across discretionary, advisory, and fund management activities.

 
 
• Mission 
 
 You will Work inside the Investments COO Investment Management (COO IM) Office and engage with our Buy-Side stakeholders, Discretionary Portfolio Managers (DPM), Product Specialists and Buy Side Analysts. 
 
 As a Portfolio Guardian, your mission is to safeguard the integrity, consistency, and client alignment of our managed portfolios across their entire lifecycle. You will act as the first line of defense for portfolio quality — ensuring that investment intent is faithfully implemented, risks are understood and controlled, and mandates remain compliant with guidelines and regulations. 
 You will also architect, build, and deploy high-impact software solutions in fast-moving, high-stakes environments inside the Investment Project and Tools team. You operate as a force multiplier across the stack — rapidly assessing the terrain, selecting the right tools, and executing with precision to deliver secure, resilient, and mission-critical capabilities. 
 
• Main responsibilities 
 
 Portfolio Guardian 
 
• Take ownership of risk/breach dashboards and other Discretionary Portfolio Management (DPM) solutions, including end-to-end oversight of underlying data-sourcing processes.

• Create solutions that support Portfolio Managers in managing their discretionary mandates.

• Manage existing discretionary models and datasets, ensuring analytics tools correctly source, reconcile, and integrate data from external providers and cross-functional systems.

• Maintain First Line of Defence (LOD1) risk controls and produce high-quality presentations and reports for DPM leadership and internal teams.

• Administer and maintain databases; collaborate closely with Quant, Performance, and Reporting teams to align models, metrics, and delivery timelines.

• Oversee and improve instrument data quality used in product factsheets (equities and bonds), including validation, enrichment, and issue remediation.
 
 Front Office Tactical Commando 
 
• Develop tactical solutions for the Investment Services floor in commando mode, in alignment with Union Bancaire Privée (UBP)’s technological framework. 

• Optimize processes and tactical tools within Investment Services. 

• Contribute to the analytical and technical analysis of business requirements

• Participate in transversal projects as part of the COO IM team.

• Lead the design and delivery of Proofs of Concept (PoCs) and orchestrate their transition to production, coordinating handover and implementation with UBP IT.
 
 
 
• Your Profile 

• You hold a degree in Economics, Business Administration, Financial Engineering, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or an equivalent quantitative/scientific discipline. 

• You bring substantial experience working with large-scale, distributed datasets, as well as demonstrated expertise in automation and process optimization. 

• You have a strong interest and relevant experience in the financial industry, with a solid understanding of investment instruments and/or applications of Artificial Intelligence within finance. 

• You are proficient in Python development and writing complex SQL queries, with experience using APIs. You have excellent knowledge of the Pandas and NumPy packages and proven experience in project development or automation, data management, and data analysis. 

• You have the confidence and credibility to challenge assumptions, question data integrity, and provide constructive, influential input. 

• You are experienced in modeling and in presenting data with data visualization tools (ideally Power BI). 

• You possess outstanding analytical, data-manipulation, and statistical-modeling skills, bringing strong quantitative rigor. 

• You operate autonomously with a high level of accountability, demonstrating exceptional attention to detail. 

• You are accustomed to managing multiple priorities in a high-paced environment. 

• You are flexible, solutions-oriented, and intellectually curious, with a proven ability to solve complex problems and adapt to evolving priorities. 

• Experience with OMS Aladdin and/or PMS AAA is a plus. 

• French / English proficiency 
 
 
• Others 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Central Vault Operations Manager
jobs.description:
Mission 
The Central Vault officer is responsible for the management, security and operations of the precious metals vault and physical certificates including security and traceability of all assets entering and leaving the vault. He is working within our institution procedural guidelines to ensure a high quality of service is consistently maintained, with optimum efficiency and all standard operating procedures and Key Performance Indicators being adhered to. He will be working closely with internal management on the strategic global growth of our projects; offering products and services combined into innovative offerings and operational knowledge.
 Essential Duties 
 Vault main tasks 
- Places, and fulfills orders from the Private Banking / Clients to maintain required levels of precious metal available in the vault
- Ensure physical delivery to Relationship manager and Clients
- Handling receipts and deliveries of physical securities and metals
- Maintains internal procedures up to date in regards with the security and regulations of the Bank’s vault. 
- Performs inventory maintenance, verification and accounting entries with reconciliation department
- Performs, confirms and maintains banking insurances according to the vault valuation.
 Trading tasks 
- Processes, monitors and validates precious metals and private placement 
- Ensure good receipt of Private Equity subscription and redemption proceeds with external funds managers
- Reconciliation and monitoring of holdings, entitlements determination, pending positions 
- Ensure daily proper record-keeping and development of new activities/business with external providers in regards with vault activities.
 
 Supervisory Responsibility 
 - Coordinates and manages Vault activities at all bank branches
- Ensure allocation to client accounts and physical securities settlement 
- Precious metals receiving - Receives and verifies vault deposits, inspects the integrity of bags and seals and notifies management of exceptions.
- Ensure follow-up with private company to maintain self-evaluation of private equity
- Perform procedures for opening and closing of operations, including vault, alarm and door duties
- Ensure that security/control procedures are correctly performed in accordance with established policies
 Specific competencies 
- Strong knowledge on financial instruments (equities, bonds, warrants, private placements, restricted securities, funds and other), and their operating flows 
- Sense of risk associated with the delivery and receipt of securities
- Flexibility, adaptation to change
- Strong analytical skills with an ability to identify problems and propose solutions.
- Sense of priorities and capacity to take decisions 
- Consistently comply with internal & external regulation
 Skills and Abilities 
- Technical knowledge of detailed domain (Compliance and legal competencies appreciated)
- CFC bank or equivalent
- Fluency in English and French
- Experience of at least 5 years in a similar position
- Discretion and excellent presentation
- MS Office / Banking software / Swift
 Business daily contacts 
- Clients
- Private Banking
- Operations department / Treasury & Trading
- Compliance department / Legal department
- IT
- Banks / Transfer Agents/ Brokers / Custodians / Funds managers
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

Essential Duties Vault main tasks - Places, and fulfills orders from the Private Banking / Clients to maintain required levels of precious metal available in the vault - Ensure physical delivery to Relationship manager and Clients - Handling receipts and deliveries of physical securities and metals - Maintains internal procedures up to date in regards with the security and regulations of the Bank’s vault. - Performs inventory maintenance, verification and accounting entries with reconciliation department - Performs, confirms and maintains banking insurances according to the vault valuation. Trading tasks - Processes, monitors and validates precious metals and private placement - Ensure good receipt of Private Equity subscription and redemption proceeds with external funds managers - Reconciliation and monitoring of holdings, entitlements determination, pending positions - Ensure daily proper record-keeping and development of new activities/business with external providers in regards with vault activities. Supervisory Responsibility - Coordinates and manages Vault activities at all bank branches - Ensure allocation to client accounts and physical securities settlement - Precious metals receiving - Receives and verifies vault deposits, inspects the integrity of bags and seals and notifies management of exceptions. - Ensure follow-up with private company to maintain self-evaluation of private equity - Perform procedures for opening and closing of operations, including vault, alarm and door duties - Ensure that security/control procedures are correctly performed in accordance with established policies Specific competencies - Strong knowledge on financial instruments (equities, bonds, warrants, private placements, restricted securities, funds and other), and their operating flows - Sense of risk associated with the delivery and receipt of securities - Flexibility, adaptation to change - Strong analytical skills with an ability to identify problems and propose solutions. - Sense of priorities and capacity to take decisions - Consistently comply with internal & external regulation Skills and Abilities - Technical knowledge of detailed domain (Compliance and legal competencies appreciated) - CFC bank or equivalent - Fluency in English and French - Experience of at least 5 years in a similar position - Discretion and excellent presentation - MS Office / Banking software / Swift Business daily contacts - Clients - Private Banking - Operations department / Treasury & Trading - Compliance department / Legal department - IT - Banks / Transfer Agents/ Brokers / Custodians / Funds managers Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Fixed Income Execution Trader

titleByLocale.it: Trader di esecuzione a reddito fisso

jobs.description:
• Mission 
 We are seeking an experienced Fixed Income Execution Trader to join our team in Geneva. For this full-time position, a solid background in bond markets, advanced execution skills and the ability to perform in a fast-paced environment is requested. Individual’s role will focus on day-to-day execution of fixed income orders in primary and secondary markets, optimizing execution strategies, and contributing to the automation of order workflows. The candidate is expected to be learning and supporting execution in other asset classes and take part to late shift coverage for US markets (up to 22:00).
 
• Main responsibilities 
• Efficient and accurate trade execution (voice and electronic) in secondary markets (Credit IG and HY, Rates, Emerging Markets, Money Markets).

• Oversee full bookbuilding processes in primary markets (publish new issues, collect subscriptions, distribute allocations)

• Play an integral part of a ‘value added’ basis to investment process by leveraging market knowledge and counterparty contacts to improve performance and influence outcomes

• Monitor and share market news and liquidity conditions with Wealth Management teams and build trust-based internal relationships

• Develop and maintain strong, transparent and constructive relationships with brokers and counterparties to obtain the best service for the bank’s clients

• Run and optimize electronic execution and RFQs via Bloomberg (e.g., IB/TSOX), MarketAxess, and Tradeweb

• Develop and implement Fixed Income execution strategies to optimize trading performance and client outcomes

• Contribute to the automation of bond order workflows and the integration of trading technologies

• Late-shift coverage (up to 22:00): mandatory multi-asset execution across Fixed Income, Equities, and ETDs

• Ensure strict compliance with internal policies and external regulatory requirements (e.g., best execution, record-keeping)
 
 

• Your Profile 
• Experience : Minimum of 5 years trading experience in Fixed Income primary and secondary markets with a proven track record 

• Product Knowledge : Deep understanding of Fixed Income products and market structure, liquidity dynamics, RFQ protocols, and new issue processes

• Trading Venues and Platforms : Hands-on experience executing via Bloomberg (IB/TSOX), MarketAxess, and Tradeweb; extensive use of InvestorAccess and DirectBooks for new issues

• Execution Skills: Strong voice trading and e-trading capabilities; familiarity with auto-routing, dealer selection, and best-ex filters.

• Languages: Fluency in English and French is a prerequisite.

• Technology: Advanced Excel; VBA and/or Python are a strong advantage, particularly for automating bond workflows and reporting.

• Versatility: Willingness to learn and execute another asset class as needed; ability to switch between tools and processes.

• Stress Management: Ability to perform under pressure in a high-stakes, fast-paced environment.

• Attention to Detail: Exceptional accuracy in execution, allocations, and post-trade reporting.

• Passion for Markets: Genuine enthusiasm for Fixed Income and trading innovation.
 
 
 
• Core Competencies 
• Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility. 

• Strong commitment to compliance with regulations and internal directives. 

• Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and team collaboration. 
 
 
 
• Others 
• Swiss Residence is required

jobs.title: WM Business Risk Manager

titleByLocale.it: Gestore del Rischio Business di WM

jobs.description:
Mission 
 The Business Risk Management team plays a key role within the Wealth Management (First Line of Defence). Business Risk Managers (BRM) are the internal referents within Front Office on the subjects of AML, CDB, Risk Appetite and KYC/KYT, and are responsible of opening client accounts and addressing transactional alerts for lower account risk categories. 
 Main responsibilities 
 
• Manage onboarding of client accounts end-to-end.

• Perform formal controls on all risk level client onboardings and review the documentation to ensure that it complies with the local and international regulations (CDB, CRS, FATCA) as well as the Bank’s directives and procedures.

• Perform reviews of onboarding file (AML, KYC, KYT, adverse news screening to mitigate risks) prior to Onboarding (lower risks) or Compliance submission (higher risks) with a strong focus on standard and medium risk relations for material checks.

• Ensure documentary accuracy and completeness of corroborations and evidences in adherence to the Bank’s qualitative risk appetite and standards. 

• Ensure that, when applicable, the SLA for onboarding is respected.

• Ensure appropriate documentation of, review and close all normal and medium risk Transactional alerts clarified by the front office in a timely manner.

• Launch Event Driven updates following transactional activity shifts and change in Circumstances in the client life cycle to keep KYC/KYT in line with the client’s activity.

• Escalate any deviations from the Bank’s standards to line management and control functions according to policies in place.

• Upon Management’s request, act as a relay of authority within the WM Markets on certain initiatives.

• Apply the changes required by Compliance Quality Control feedbacks in a continuous improvement manner.

• Digital and manual input in the Bank’s systems relating to account openings.

• Collaborate actively across markets with BRMs and Senior BRMs to balance workloads, protect deadlines, and leverage specific market expertise when required.

• Any other task required by Management relating to the Mission of the employee.
 
 Your Profile 
 
• University degree (Bachelor) or a qualification considered equivalent. 

• Private Banking, Central file or WM Compliance or equivalent background with strong understanding of CDB, AML, tax regulations and sanctions matters. Any previous experience with MEA markets is an added value. 

• Min 5 years of experience. 

• Strong interpersonal and communication skills with the flexibility to seamlessly interact with different levels of seniority and authority. 

• Good command of the Bank’s systems and MS Office suite. Knowledge of Power-BI is a plus. 

• Fluent in French and English. Any additional language skill is an added value, in particular Arabic. 

• Solution oriented team player, capable of working independently. 

• Stress resistant, reliable, and flexible. 

• Able and willing to learn, acquire new skills and grow in an agile organization. 
 
 Others 
 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Graduate Programme Hong Kong, 2026, Wealth Management

titleByLocale.it: Programma di laurea Hong Kong, 2026, Gestione della ricchezza
titleByLocale.en: Graduate Programme Hong Kong, 2026, Wealth Management
titleByLocale.de: Graduate Programme Hong Kong, 2026, Wealth Management
titleByLocale.fr: Graduate Programme Hong Kong, 2026, Wealth Management

jobs.description:
• Permanent position with a 12-months rotational program within different teams to discover different activities, as well as build expertise and professional skills. 
• Rotation 1 – [Business Management Team for 9 months] 

• Rotation 2 – [Investment Services Team for 3 months]
 
 

• During the program, under supervision of the Head of CE Office as mentor, the Graduate is expected to: 
• Get familiar with the organization structure, the roles and responsibilities of different functional teams and the usage of the Bank’s core systems

• Rotate between business management, front office sales team and Investment Services team to learn the key tasks and responsibilities
 

• The rotation in the business management team will be focus on: 
• Support project management: Learn to collaborate with the team on various projects relates to process improvement, operational efficiency, and Front Office risk management

• Coordinate meeting and presentation, including preparing meeting agenda, coordinating schedules, and taking meeting minutes

• Housekeeping: progress and status tracking in client portfolio review, cross-border travel report, and maintain the Business Introducer list

• Daily routine monitoring task in the Business Management Team

• Coordinate in project UAT / Testing arrangement

• The Graduate will be attached to Assistants of Private Banker to learn the daily tasks carried out in the Front Office, e.g. selling process, order taking from client, handling remittance, account maintenance etc.
 

• Main responsibilities in the Investment Services Team: 
• The Graduate will be attached to various investment/product specialist to learn about the products offering from the bank, e.g. Equity, Fixed Income, Mutual Funds, Structured Products and Alternative Investments, and the key products features and characteristics of these products

• The Graduate will learn from the Advisory team on engaging private client for investment products
 
 
 

• At the end of all rotations, the Graduate is expected to be fully trained in: 
• Front Office practices and requirements

• Products and services offered in Private Banking

• Business Management for Private Banking Front Office

• Professional organizational behaviour and conduct in an international Private Bank
 
 

• Starting Date: September 2026
 

 Rationale – Why Business Management Team in Private Bank? 
 Joining Business Management team in a Private Bank can be a valuable opportunity to kickstart the Graduate’s career in the Finance industry. For example:
 
• Practical experience and exposure: Private Banks are at the forefront of the financial industry and the exposures in the Business Management team offers the Graduate get into the inner working of how a private bank operates. The Graduate will gain a comprehensive understanding of the banking operations, risk management, compliance, and client services. This exposure will help the Graduate develop a solid foundation of knowledge and provide a first-hand understanding of the dynamics of the financial sector. 
 

• Skill developments: The internship in a business management team allows the Graduate to develop and enhance essential skills that are highly valued in the finance industry. For example, project management skills, problem solving skills, communication skills, and teamwork etc. 
 

• Networking opportunities: Due to the nature of Business Management team which connects the Front Offices to all the functional team in the middle and back offices, it opens doors to networking opportunities. The Graduate will have the chance to interact with professionals from various departments within the bank, including Bankers, senior executives, investment advisors, COO and Compliance etc. Building relationship with these important individuals can be invaluable, as they can provide guidance, mentorship, and potential future job opportunities. 
 
 Candidates with, at minimum, a bachelor's degree (or in the final-year study) and, no more than 12 months of full-time post-graduate work experience are welcome to apply.
